translations.source: Dafin cizon maciji Bayani: Dafin cizon maciji cuta ce mai yiwuwar cutar da rayuwa wadda ke haifar da guba idan mutum ya gamu da cikin cizon maciji. Haka kuma cutar dafin cizon tana iya samuwa ta hanyar fesa yawu da wani nau’in macizai ke yi a cikin idanuwa a matsayin matakin kare kansu. Rashin ƙoƙarin da aka yi a baya don shawo kan cutar dafin maciji ya haifar da rashin cikakku da ingantattun bayanan yaɗuwar cututtuka. Yawancin waɗanda suka taɓa samun cizon maciji ba sa zuwa cibiyoyin lafiya ko asibitoci; maimakon haka sukan dogara da magungunan gargajiya. Duk da cewa bayanan da aka tattara sun nuna cewa maciji yana sarar mutane miliyan 4.5 zuwa 5.4 a duk shekara. Daga cikin waɗannan, miliyan 1.8 zuwa 2.7 suna samun rashin lafiya kuma 81 000 zuwa 138 000 suna mutuwa daga saboda wannan cizo. Mutanen da suka fi hatsarin fuskantar wannan sun haɗa da masu aikin gona na karkara, makiyaya, masunta, mafarauta, yara masu aiki, mutanen da ke zaune a gidajen da ba a gina su da kyau ba, da kuma waɗanda ke da ƙarancin damar samun ilimi da kiwon lafiya. Kamuwa da cutar da mace-mace na faruwa a-kai-a-kai tsakanin matasa sannan yara kanana sun fi fama da yawan mace-mace. Bugu da ƙari, mata ba sa samun kulawar asibiti a wasu al'adu kuma mata masu juna biyu sun fi zama masu rauni a wannan fannin.
translations.reference: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments. However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Scabies Information:** Scabies is a life-threatening disease that causes itching, occurring when a person comes into contact with scabies mites. Scabies can also be contracted through the feces (excrement) that some mites release in burrows as a defense mechanism. Past efforts to control scabies have resulted in a lack of complete and reliable data on disease transmission. Most people who have scabies do not seek medical care or visit hospitals; instead, they rely on traditional remedies. However, collected data indicates that scabies affects 4.5 to 5.4 million people annually. Of these, 1.8 to 2.7 million experience illness, and 81,000 to 138,000 die from this infestation. People at higher risk of contracting scabies include rural farmers, herders, livestock keepers, miners, child laborers, people living in poorly constructed homes, and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Scabies is frequently observed among young people, and young children are particularly vulnerable. Furthermore, women do not receive hospital care in some cultures, and pregnant women are more vulnerable in this area.

translations.source: Rikicin da ke ci gaba da hana samun kulawar lafiya, ingantaccen maganin riga-kafi a yankuna da yawa, musamman ma yankin kudu da hamadar Sahara, wani abu ne da ke ba da gudummawa wajen yiwuwar neman taimako ta hanyar maganin gargajiya. Alamominta: Cizo ko fesa dafin da macizai masu dafi ke yi na iya haifar da mummunan yanayin gaggawa na rashin lafiya. Saka dafin nau'ikan macizai daban-daban na iya haifar da alamomi daban-daban, sai dai wasu sun fi wasu tsanani. Wannan ya sa shirye-shiryen neman ainihin maganin riga-kafi ya zama cike da matsaloli a halin yanzu. Dafin cizon maciji na iya haifar da shanyewar jiki mai tsanani wanda zai iya hana numfashi, kuma yana haifar da matsananciyar kulawar likita cikin gaggawa. Sannan kuma mutane na iya fuskantar matsalar zubar jini mai kisa ko matsalar ƙoda da ba za a iya maganinta ba. Har ila yau, za a iya samun mummunar lalacewar tsoka, wanda zai haifar da nakasu na dindindin har ma da yanke gaɓoɓin jiki. Yara sum fi samun kansu cikin wannan yanayi mai tsanani saboda rashin nauyin jiki. Yawancin mace-mace da munanan lamurran saran maciji ana iya yin riga-kafinsu gaba ɗaya ta hanyar samar da amintattun magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta da kuma samun dama, musamman a wuraren da ke da haɗarin macizai. Magani masu inganci na macizai su ne kawai ingantaccen magani don hanawa ko karkatar da mafi yawan illolin cizon maciji.
translations.reference: An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical. People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"The ongoing crisis hindering access to healthcare, and effective snakebite treatment in many regions, particularly in the sub-Saharan African region, contributes to the possibility of seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or stings from snakes, especially venomous snakes, can cause severe and urgent health conditions. Bites from different types of snakes can cause varying symptoms, although some are more severe than others. This makes establishing effective snakebite treatment programs challenging at present. A snake bite can cause severe systemic poisoning that can lead to respiratory failure, requiring intensive medical care in an emergency. People may also experience life-threatening bleeding or uncontrollable seizures. Severe tissue damage can also occur, potentially leading to permanent disability or even amputation. Children are more likely to find themselves in this critical situation due to their lower body weight. Most snakebite-related deaths and serious complications can be entirely prevented by providing safe antivenom and ensuring access, especially in areas with a high risk of snakes. Effective snake antivenoms are the only proven treatment for preventing or mitigating most of the complications of snakebite."

translations.source: Yin magani: Magungunan dafin maciji sun kasance suna da inganci wajen hanawa ko sauƙaƙa mafi yawan illolin cutar saran maciji kuma suna cikin jerin mahimman magunguna na WHO. Samuwar waɗannan magungunan kashe dafin, tare da wayar da kan jama'a kan hanyoyin riga-kafin farko a tsakanin al'ummomi da ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya, su ne mafi kyawun hanyoyin da za a iya maganin mace-mace saboda cizon maciji. Bayan saran maciji da ake zargin yana da dafi, to a bi waɗannan matakan: Nan da nan mutum ya tashi daga wajen da aka yi masa cizon. A cire duk wani abu da zai iya tamke kewayen sashen jikin da aka ciz don gujewa cutarwa idan aka samu kumburi. Ka kwantar da hankalin wanda abin aka cizan, saboda yawancin saran maciji ba ya kashe mutum nan-take. A hana mutum motsi gaba ɗaya kuma a kai shi asibiti da wuri-wuri Wasu lokutan idan aka ɗan mammatsa wurin cizon, ana iya dacewa. A guji hanyoyin taimakon gaggawa na gargajiya ko magungunan itace. Ana iya ba da paracetamol don kashe zafin wajen (wanda zai iya zama mai tsanani).
translations.reference: Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs. Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe).
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Treatment:** Antivenom medications are effective in preventing or alleviating most of the effects of viper bites and are included in the WHO's list of essential medicines. The availability of these antivenoms, along with public awareness campaigns on primary prevention methods among communities and healthcare workers, are the best ways to treat viper bites. After a suspected viper bite with envenomation, follow these steps: Immediately move the person away from the location of the bite. Remove any items that may constrict the area of the bitten body part to prevent swelling. Reassure the bitten person, as most viper bites are not immediately fatal. Prevent the person from moving at all and transport them to the hospital as soon as possible. In some cases, gentle cleaning of the bite area may be appropriate. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal remedies. Paracetamol can be administered to reduce the pain at the site (which can be severe).

translations.source: Mutun yana iya yin amai, don haka a sanya shi a gefen hagunsu don daina aman. A kula da yadda yake numfashi a hankali kuma ku a kasance a shirye don farfado shi idan akwai buƙatar hakan.
translations.reference: Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

"A person can faint, so place them on their side to prevent choking. Monitor their breathing carefully and be prepared to perform rescue breaths if necessary."



**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **"Faint"** is a common and accurate translation of "yana iya yin amai" in this context.
*   **"On their side"** is the standard recommendation for someone who has fainted.
*   **"Choking"** is the best translation for "don daina aman" in this situation.
*   **"Rescue breaths"** is a clear way to convey "farfado shi" in the context of someone who may not be breathing properly.

translations.source: Cutar tsutsotsi Bayani: Cututtukan tsutsotsi (wato STH a taƙaice) suna daga cikin cututtukan da aka fi sani a duk duniya kuma suna shafar al'ummomin da suka fi talauci da rashin ƙarfi. Suna samuwa ne ta hanyar ƙwai da ke cikin najasar ɗan'adam wanda hakan ke gurɓata ƙasa a wuraren da tsaftar muhalli ta ti ƙaranci. Manyan nau'ukan da ke cutar da mutane su ne roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) da hookworm (Necator Americanus da Ancylostoma duodenale). Tsutsotsi na hanji suna fitar da alamomi iri-iri da suka shafi hanji (zawo, ciwon ciki), rashin lafiya da mutuwar jiki. Hookworms suna haifar da asarar jini na hanji na yau da kullun wanda ke haifar da cutar da ake kira anemia a Turance. Ana samar da waɗannan tsutsotsi ne ta hanyar ƙwai waɗanda ke fitowa a cikin najasar mutanen da suka kamu da cutar. Tsutsotsi na manya suna rayuwa ne a cikin hanji inda suke samar da dubban ƙwai kowace rana. A wuraren da ba su da isasshen tsafta, waɗannan ƙwai suna gurɓata ƙasa. Wannan na iya faruwa ta hanyoyi da yawa: ana cin wannan ƙwan da ke maƙala da kayan lambu ne a lokacin da ba a dafa kayan lambun da kyau ba, ko ba a wanke ko an ɓare bawon da kyai ba;
translations.reference: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness. Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Intestinal Worms: Explanation**

Intestinal worm diseases (commonly referred to as STH – Soil-Transmitted Helminths – in short) are among the most prevalent diseases worldwide and affect the most vulnerable and impoverished communities. They are transmitted through eggs present in human feces, which contaminate the soil in areas with poor sanitation. The main types that infect humans are roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), whipworm (Trichuris trichiura), and hookworm (Necator Americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a variety of symptoms affecting the intestines (pain, abdominal discomfort), weakness, and weight loss. Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss, leading to a condition called anemia in English. These worms are produced by eggs that are excreted in the feces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestines, producing thousands of eggs each day. In areas with inadequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: the eggs are ingested when eating vegetables that have not been properly washed or cooked, or when manure has not been properly composted.

translations.source: ana cin ƙwan a gurɓataccen ruwa; yaran da ke wasa a gurɓataccen wuri suna cin ƙwan ne idan sun sanya hannayensu a cikin bakinsu ba tare da wankewa ba. Alamunta: kamuwa da cutar yana da alaƙa da adadin tsutsotsi da suka yi wa mutum katutu. Mutanen da ke ɗauke tsutsotsin ƙaɗan yawanci cutar ba ta kama su. Idan kuma suka yi yawa, suna iya haifar da alamomi iri-iri da suka danganci hanji (zawo da ciwon ciki), rashin nuna cewa mutum yana cin abinci mai gina jiki, raunin jiki da rashin girma da haɓakar jiki. Lokacin da cutar ta yi tsanani sosai, tana iya haifar da toshewar hanji wanda ya kamata a ce an yi tiyata. S. stercoralis na iya haifar da cututtuka na fara da hanji kuma an san yana da alaƙa da rashin abinci mai gina jiki na yau da kullum a tsakanin yara. Idan ma mai ɗauke da cutar garkuwar jikinsa ta ragu, ƙwayar cuta na iya karaɗe masa jiki, wadda tana iya kisa a koyaushe idan ba a yi magani da sauri ba kuma sau da yawa mutum yana iya mutuwa duk da an masa jinya. A shekarar 2001, wakilai a Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya sun amince da wani kuduri (WHA54.19) wanda yake kira ga ƙasashe masu fama da cutar da su fara yaƙar tsutsotsi, musamman schistosomiasis da helminths. Dabarar yaƙar cututtukan tsutsotsin ita ce sarrafa cututtuka ta hanyar jinya na lokaci-lokaci na mutanen da ke fama da abin waɗanda kuma ke zaune a yankunan da ke fama da cutar.
translations.reference: eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development. Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

"People are swallowing worms in contaminated water; children playing in contaminated areas swallow worms when they put their hands in their mouths without washing. Symptoms: infection is related to the number of worms that infest a person. People who carry few worms are usually not infected. If there are many, they can cause various symptoms related to intestinal problems (bloating and abdominal pain), lack of appetite, weakness, and stunted growth and development. When the infection is very severe, it can cause intestinal obstruction which may require surgery. *S. stercoralis* can cause intestinal illnesses and is known to be associated with a lack of nutritious food among children. If the infected person's immunity decreases, the parasite can multiply in the body, which can be fatal if not treated quickly and often a person can die despite receiving injections. In 2001, representatives at the World Health Assembly adopted a resolution (WHA54.19) calling on countries struggling with parasitic diseases to begin fighting worms, especially schistosomiasis and helminths. The strategy for fighting worm diseases is to control the diseases through periodic deworming of people suffering from them and those living in areas affected by the diseases."

translations.source: Mutanen da za su iya daukar cutar: Yaran da ba su shiga makaranta ba tukunna Yara ƴan makaranta matan da suka kai shekarun haihuwa (ciki har da mata masu juna biyu da ke tsakanin wata 6 zuwa 9 da mata masu shayarwa) mutane a wasu sana'o'i masu haɗarin ɗaukar wannan cutar akwai masu noman ganyen shayi ko masu haƙar ma'adinai. Magani: Magungunan da WHO ta ba da shawarar aiki da su -albendazole (400 MG) da mebendazole (500 MG) - suna da tasiri, ba su da tsada kuma ga sauƙin gane yadda za a yi amfani da shi ko ga ma'aikatan da ba likita ba (misali malamai). An duba magungunan sosai ta hanyar gwaji mai yawa kuma an yi amfani da su a kan miliyoyin mutane, kuma ba a ga wata illa tare da su ba. Duka albendazole da mebendazole ana ba da su gudummawa ga ma'aikatun lafiya na ƙasa ta ƙarƙashin WHO a duk ƙasashen da ke fama da cutar don kula da duk yaran da suka isa shiga makaranta. Ivermectin wanda ake sa ran zai rage yaɗuwar S. stercoralis zai kasance a farashi mai araha daga 2021.
translations.reference: People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners. TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**People at Risk of Contracting the Disease:**

*   Children who do not attend school
*   School children
*   Women who have reached reproductive age (including pregnant women between 6 and 9 months and breastfeeding mothers)
*   People in certain occupations with a higher risk of contracting this disease, such as tea plantation workers or miners.

**Treatment:**

The medications recommended by WHO – albendazole (400 MG) and mebendazole (500 MG) – are effective, inexpensive, and easy to administer, even by non-medical personnel (e.g., teachers). These medications have been extensively tested through numerous trials and have been used on millions of people, and no adverse effects have been observed. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national health ministries by WHO in all countries affected by the disease to treat all children who are old enough to attend school. Ivermectin, which is expected to reduce the spread of *S. stercoralis*, will be available at an affordable price from 2021.



**Notes on the Translation:**

*   I've tried to maintain the clarity and directness of the original Hausa text.
*   *S. stercoralis* is the scientific name for the parasite causing the disease, and I've kept it in Latin.

translations.source: Haihuwar jariri a mace Bayani: Jaririn da ya mutu bayan makonni 28 a ciki, kafin ko lokacin haihuwa, ana ƙirga shi a matsayin wanda aka haifa a mace. Ana haihuwar jarirai kusan miliyan 2 a mace a kowace shekara - jariri ɗaya ke nan a cikin duk daƙika 16. Fiye da kashi 40 cikin 100 na jariran da ake haifa a mace suna mutuwa ne a lokacin naƙuda – asarar da za a iya guje wa ta hanyar ingantaccen tsari da nuna kulawa yayin haihuwa, ciki har da sa ido na yau da kullum da ba da kulawar haihuwa ta gaggawa lokacin da ya kamata. Mutuwar jarirai a lokacin juna biyu ko lokacin haihuwa wani bala'i ne wanda ba a magance shi ba a cikin ajandar duniya, manufofi da shirye-shiryen da aka ba wa kuɗi. Ana samun kaɗuwar zuciya, musamman ga mata da danginsu, kamar shiga damuwa lokacin jego, matsalolin kuɗi, da kuma nuna ƙyama da ƙiyayya. A shekarar 2014, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince da Tsarin Hidima ga Jarirai (ENAP), wanda ya haɗa da burin rage adadin mace-macen jarirai a cikin mahaifa da guda 12 ko ƙasa da haka a wata na huɗu zuwa shida lokacin rainon ciki a jimillar haihuwa 1,000 a kowace ƙasa nan da 2030. A shekarar 2021, ƙasashe 139 galibi masu ƙarfin tattalin arziki da masu tattalin arziki dama-dama sun cimma wannan manufa, amma ƙasashe 56 ba za su cimma wannan manufa ta ENAP ba nan da shekarar 2030 idan ba a ƙara yin kokari ba. Idan aka ci gaba a haka, za a haifi jarirai miliyan 15.9 a mace; kuma kusan rabin waɗannan (watau miliyan 7.7, 48%) duk a yankin ƙasashen kudu da Sahara. Abubuwan da ke jawo shi: Idan akwai ingantacciyar kulawar lafiya tun daga lokacin rainon ciki da haihuwa, ana iya kauce wa mafi yawan haihuwar jarirai a mace. Manyan abubuwan da ke haifar da haihuwar jarirai a mace sun haɗa da matsalolin rainon ciki da haihuwa, doguwar naƙuda, cututtuka kamar zazzabin cizon sauro, cutar sifilis da cutar sida, hawan jini, da ciwon sukari, da hana girman jaririn da ke ciki. Matsalolin da ake haifar mutum da su suna haddasa ƙasa da kaso 10 na mutuwar jarirai kafin a haife su kamar yadda aka ruwaito a ƙasashe masu ƙarfin tattalin arziki kuma ba a san ainihin adadin ba a ƙasashe masu ƙarancin tattalin arziki saboda rashin iya binciko waɗannan lamurran.
translations.reference: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030. In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Stillbirth: An Explanation**

A stillbirth is defined as the death of a fetus after 28 weeks of gestation, either before or during labor. Approximately 2 million babies are stillborn each year – one baby every 16 minutes. Over 40% of stillbirths occur during the third trimester – a loss that could be prevented through improved care and monitoring during pregnancy and labor, including regular monitoring and providing emergency obstetric care when necessary. Stillbirths during the second trimester or at birth remain a neglected tragedy in the global agenda, policies, and funded programs. The grief experienced, especially by women and their families, can be akin to entering a period of depression, financial difficulties, and feelings of shame and guilt.

In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes the goal of reducing the number of stillbirths to 12 or fewer per 1,000 total births by the fourth to sixth month of pregnancy in every country by 2030. In 2021, 139 mostly low- and lower-middle-income countries achieved this target, but 56 countries will not achieve the ENAP target by 2030 unless greater efforts are made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million stillbirths will occur; and nearly half (i.e., 7.7 million, 48%) of these will be in sub-Saharan Africa.

**Causes:**

Most stillbirths can be avoided with improved healthcare from early pregnancy through childbirth. Major factors contributing to stillbirths include complications during pregnancy and labor, prolonged labor, infections such as malaria, syphilis, and HIV/AIDS, hypertension, diabetes, and fetal growth restriction. Human-related factors cause less than 10% of stillbirths before birth, as reported in high-income countries, and the actual number is unknown in low-income countries due to a lack of investigation into these cases.

translations.source: Abubuwan da suka danganci shekarun uwa da kuma shan taba suna iya ƙara yiwuwar kamuwa da cututtukan da ke da alaƙa da jego da kuma haihuwar jarirai a mace. Ana iya kauce wa haihuwar jarirai a mace ta hanyar tsarin iyali don hana samin haihuwar da ba a buƙata, samun cikakken kiwon lafiya da abinci mai gina jiki kafin da lokacin rainon ciki, samun ingantacciyar kulawa yayin haihuwa, ciki har da samar da ƙwararrun ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya, kamar da ungozoma. Maganin cutar sifilis a lokacin rainon ciki shi ma zai iya hana haihuwar kimanin jarirai 200 000 a mace, yayin da saka idanu kan bugun zuciya da sa ido a kan aiki da kuma kawo ɗauki lokacin da ake buƙata suna da mahimmanci don kauce wa mutuwar jarirai da ke cikin mahaifansu guda 832,000 tare da rage mutuwar jarirai. Haihuwar jariri a mace har yanzu tana da alaƙa mai ƙarfi da munanan al'amurran lafiya na zamantakewa da tattalin arziki. Riga-kafi da nuna kulawa ta hanyar kai agaji suna buƙatar a haɗa su a yi amfani da su a fannin kula da lafiyar mata da sauran fannoni. Wannan ya haɗa da kulawa da tallafi a yayin mutuwa. Sauraron abubuwan da suka faru mata da al'ummominsu da suka fito yana da mahimmanci don taimakawa wajen magance matsalolin ƙyama da ke da alaƙa da haihuwar jarirai a mace. Amsar WHO: Ana buƙatar ƙara zage damtse don ganin an sanya yaƙi da haihuwar jarirai a mace cikin ajandoji na duniya da na ƙasa don ingantaccen kiwon lafiya ga mata, matasa, da jarirai. Ta ƙarƙashin shirin Dabarun Lafiyar Mata, Yara da Matasa a Faɗin Diniya 2016-2030 da amfani da shawarwarin kowane Tsarin Hidima wa Jarirai a (2014), WHO na aiki don kawo ƙarshen haifuwar jarirai mace, a matsayin wani ɓangare na ƙoƙarin inganta lafiyar mata masu juna biyu, Jariri, yaro da lafiyar matasa. Yawancin jariran da ake haihuwa a mace ba sa samun takardar shaidar haihuwa kuma ba su da rajista.
translations.reference: Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond. This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"Factors related to maternal age and smoking can increase the risk of developing conditions associated with maternal and infant mortality. Maternal mortality can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, receiving adequate nutrition and healthcare before and during pregnancy, receiving quality care during childbirth, including the provision of skilled health workers, such as midwives. Treating syphilis during pregnancy can also prevent approximately 200,000 maternal deaths, while monitoring fetal heart rate and activity and timely delivery are essential to prevent the deaths of an estimated 832,000 infants in their mothers' wombs and reduce infant mortality. Maternal mortality is still strongly linked to severe social and economic consequences. Increased awareness and care through humanitarian aid need to be integrated and utilized in the field of women's health and other areas. This includes care and support during bereavement. Listening to the experiences of women and their communities is essential to help address the challenges related to maternal mortality. WHO Response: Increased commitment is needed to ensure that the fight against maternal mortality is included in global and national agendas for improved health for women, adolescents, and infants. Under the Global Strategy for Women’s, Children’s and Adolescents’ Health 2016-2030 and utilizing the recommendations of every Newborn Care System (2014), WHO is working to end maternal mortality, as part of efforts to improve the health of women during pregnancy, newborns, children, and adolescents. Many of the births occurring in maternal mortality do not receive birth certificates and are unregistered."

translations.source: Inganta tsarin bayar da rahoton haihuwa da mutuwar jarirai al'amari ne na 'yancin ɗan'adam kuma abu ne da ake buƙata don rage haifar jarirai a mace. Bugu da ƙari, akwai buƙatar ƙarfafa tsarin bayanan kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum don samar da sahihin rahoton haihuwa don sa ido da haɓaka ingancin ba da kulawar lafiya. WHO ta ba da kayan aiki don taimaka wa ƙasashe su inganta ɗaukar ƙididdigarsu game da haihuwa da kuma mutuwar jarirai. WHO ta kuma ba da hanyoyi don taimaka wa ƙasashe yin nazari da bincika mutuwar mutane don su iya ba da shawararda aiwatar da mafita don hana irin waɗannan a nan gaba da kuma samar da tsarin tantance mutuwa kafin haihuwa (ICD-PM). WHO da UNICEF suna goyan bayan kira ga matakin gama kai don kawo ƙarshen haifuwar jarirai a mace ta hanyar: ƙara wayar da kai da rage jin ƙyama taimaka wa mata da iyalan da aka mutu aka bari inganta harkar kiwon lafiya kallon matsalar mutuwar jarirai kafin haihuwa abin da ya shafi ƙasa da al'umma yunƙurin ƙara ƙaimi wajen ɗaukar ƙididdigar mutuwar jarirai a mace domin tabbatar da faruwar lamarin da kuma sanin halin da ake ciiki.
translations.reference: Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by: increasing awareness and reducing stigma supporting bereaved women and families strengthening health systems to provide high-quality care nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets improving measurement of stillbirths to enhance evidence and knowledge.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"Improving the system of reporting births and deaths of infants is a matter of human rights and a necessary measure to reduce infant mortality among women. Furthermore, there is a need to strengthen routine health data systems to provide accurate birth reports for monitoring and improving the quality of healthcare delivery. WHO has provided tools to help countries improve their data collection on births and infant deaths. WHO has also provided methods to help countries analyze and investigate deaths so they can provide recommendations for implementing solutions to prevent such occurrences in the future and establish a system for classifying deaths before birth (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF support a call to action for the global commitment to end infant mortality among women by: increasing awareness and reducing stigma, helping women and families affected by death to improve their health practices, viewing the problem of infant mortality before birth as a national and community issue, and making efforts to increase accuracy in collecting data on infant mortality among women to confirm the occurrence of the event and understand the current situation."

translations.source: Kayan aikin jinya da magunguna marasa inganci da na bogi Bayyani: Kimanin mutane biliyan biyu a duniya ba su da damar samun ainihin magunguna, alluran riga-kafi, na'urorin likitanci da suka hada da na bincika rashin lafiya, da sauran kayayyakin kiwon lafiya, waɗanda saboda rashinsu sai dai kawai a yi amfani da marasa inganci kuma na bogi. Wannan matsala tana ta ƙaruwa ne yayin da salsalar samar da kayayyaki na duniya ke ƙara cika da sarƙaƙƙiya, ma'ana kayayyakin da aka ƙera a wata ƙasa ana iya tattara ninke su cikin fakiti a wata ƙasa kuma a tura su don a tallata ko a sayar da su ga masu siye a wata ƙasa daban. Bunƙasar kasuwancin zamani ya taimaka wajen wannan al'amari ta hanyar sauƙaƙawa wajen siyan magungunan ta intanet, yawanci ta hannun waɗanda ba su cancanta ba. . Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta bayyana wannan matsalar da ɗaya daga cikin matsalolin lafiya da za a fuskanta nan da shekara goma, inda aka yi hasashen samun ɗaya daga cikin magunguna goma yakasance ba shi da inganci ko na jabu a ƙasashen da ke da ƙaranci da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga. Babu wata ƙasa da wannan al'amari bai shafa ba, in Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta samu rahotanni game da magunguna da allurori marasa inganci ko na jabu da kuma gwaje-gwajen cututtuka daga kome lungu da saƙo na duniya. Wannan lamari na yin jabu ka iya shafar kowane nau'i na magani, kama daga magungunan kansa masu tsada zuwa magungunan kashe zafi masu sauƙi. ƙasashen da cikin Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya sun amince da a ɗauki babban mataki da zai mayar da hankali wajen kawarwa da ganowa da kuma ɗaukar mataki wajen samun ƙaruwar kaiwa ga ingantacce, amintacce kuma gangariyar magani. Tasiri: Magunguna marasa inganci ko na jabu na haifar da matsaloli da dama ga ɗaiɗaikun mutane da iyalai da suke amfani da su da ma al'umma da tsarin kula da lafiya da kuma hanyoyin rarraba su. Yayin da maganunguna ba su yi aiki ba, za su gaza ne wajen yin maganin cuta, lamarin da ka iya haifar da tirjiyar ƙwayoyin cuta da ɓarnar dukiya da kuma daina amincewa da da magunguna da kuma masu samar da kulawa ta lafiya.
translations.reference: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified. No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Substandard and Counterfeit Medicines and Medical Devices: An Explanation**

Approximately two billion people worldwide lack access to genuine medicines, vaccines, medical devices including diagnostic tools, and other healthcare products. Due to this lack of access, they are often forced to use substandard and counterfeit products. This problem is increasing as the global supply chain becomes more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may have their components sourced from another, then assembled and sold to consumers in a different country. Modern commerce has facilitated this issue by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often through unauthorized channels.

The World Health Organization (WHO) has identified this as one of the major health challenges to be faced within the next ten years, estimating that one in ten medicines could be substandard or counterfeit in low- and middle-income countries. No country is immune to this issue; the WHO has received reports of substandard or counterfeit medicines and medical products from all over the world. This falsification can affect any type of medication, ranging from expensive specialty drugs to over-the-counter pain relievers.

United Nations member states have agreed to take significant steps to focus on prevention, detection, and taking action to improve access to quality, safe, and effective medicines.

**Impact:** Substandard or counterfeit medicines and medical devices cause numerous problems for individuals and families who use them, as well as for the healthcare system and distribution channels. When medicines do not work, they fail to treat the illness, which can lead to the development of drug resistance, waste of resources, and a loss of trust in medicines and healthcare providers.

translations.source: Wannan ka iya haifar da mummunan tasiri a kan tsarin tattalin arziki da zamantakewa, wanda shi kuma zai taimaka wajen daina samar da magungunan da ƙarin kashe kuɗin ga ɗaiɗaikun mutane da kuma tsarin lafiya na ƙasa. Wannan tasiri ka iya kaiwa ga marasa lafiya, a yayin da aka samu tarnaƙi wajen kaiwa wa ga ingantacce kuma amintaccen magani da rashin shugabanci mai nagarta da kuma rauni wajen amfani da fasaha. Kodayake wannan matsala ta shafi kafatanin ƙasashen duniya, to amma a ƙasashen da da suke da rauni ko rashin tsarin sa-ido, sun fi samun magunguna marasa inganci ko na jabu. Sakamakon yadda duniya ke ci gaba da kasancewa tamkar ɗaya, babu wata ƙasa da ke da ƙarfin tattalin arzikin da za ta tunkari wannan matsalar ita kaɗai. Ta hanyar tsare-tsare na duniya da kuma hukumomi irin su Hukumar Sa-ido ta Hukumar Llafiya ta Duniya da kuma Kwamitin Mambobin ƙasashe na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya, Mabobin ƙasashe na aiki kafaɗa da kafaɗa domin hana da gano da kuma ɗaukar matakai a kan magaunguna marasa inganci ko jabu.
translations.reference: This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism, Member States are working together to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"This can have a devastating impact on the economic and social system, which will also hinder the production of medicines and lead to increased costs for individuals and the national healthcare system. This impact can affect the sick, while access to improved and reliable treatment is hampered by a lack of effective leadership and vulnerability in the use of technology. Although this problem affects all countries, those that are weak or lack regulatory systems are more likely to encounter substandard or counterfeit medicines. Given that the world continues to be as one, no country is strong enough to tackle this problem alone. Through global planning and authorities such as the Regulatory Sub-Committee of the World Health Organization and the Committee of Member States of the World Health Organization, Member States are working hand-in-hand to prevent, detect, and take action against substandard or counterfeit medicines."

translations.source: Magance ƙunar-baƙin-wake/ksan-kai Sharhi: Fiye da mutane 700 000 ne suke rasa rayukansu a kowace shekara a sanadiyyar ƙunar-baƙin-wake/kisan-kai. Bisa faruwar kowane ƙunar-baƙin-wake, akwai hasashen yin ƙunar-baƙin-wake fiye da 20. ƙunar-baƙin-wake/kisank-kai kan iya aukuwa a kowane mataki na rayuwa, sannan kuma a kowane ɓangare na duniya. A 2019, ƙunar-baƙin-wake shi ne na huɗu a cikin abubuwan da suke haifar da mutuwa a tsakanin 'yan shekara 15-29 a duniya da kuma kaso 77% na ƙunar-baƙin-wake/kisan-kai na faruwa ne a tsakanin ƙasashe masu mafi qaranci da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga. Kimanin kaso 20% na ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai a duniya, na faruwa ne sakamakon shan guba bisa ra'in ƙashi-kai. A yayin da aka tabbatar da danganata ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai da yanayin matsalar ƙwaƙwalwa (Musamman damuwa da shan barasa ba tare da ƙaƙƙautawa ba) da kuma yunƙurin yin ƙunar-baƙin-wake/kisank-kai a baya a ƙasashen da yawan kuɗin shiga, inda yawan yin ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai ke faruwa a lokacin rigingimu. Abubuwan da ke haifar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai sun haɗa da karaya da damuwa da nuna bambanci da rabuwar wata alaƙa da matsalar kuɗi da matsanancin ciwo da cuta da keta haddi da rigingimu da sauran abubuwa da suka shafi keta haddin ɗan'adam. Matsalar yanayin hauka da na ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai shi ne mutane suna yin tunanin ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai ba neman taimako ba. ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai ko ƙoƙarin yin hakan yana da mummunan tasiri a kan iyalai da abokai da abokan aiki da al'umma gaba ɗaya.
translations.reference: Suicide prevention Overview: More than 700 000 people die by suicide every year. For each suicide, there are an estimated more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15–29-year-olds globally, and 77% of all suicides happened in low- and middle-income countries. Almost 20% of global suicides were due to pesticide self-poisoning. While the link between suicide and mental health conditions (in particular, depression and alcohol use disorders) and a previous suicide attempt is well established in high-income countries, many suicides happen impulsively in moments of crisis. Further risk factors for suicide include experience of loss, loneliness, discrimination, a relationship break-up, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and conflict or other humanitarian emergencies. Stigma around mental health conditions and suicide means many people thinking about suicide do not seek help. Suicides and suicide attempts have a ripple effect that impacts on families, friends, colleagues, communities and societies.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Addressing Suicide/Self-Harm**

**Explanation:** Over 700,000 people lose their lives each year due to suicide/self-harm. For every suicide that occurs, there is an estimated additional 20 suicide attempts. Suicide/self-harm can occur at any stage of life and in every part of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15-29 year olds globally, and approximately 77% of suicides occur in low- and middle-income countries. Roughly 20% of suicides worldwide occur as a result of poisoning due to self-harm.

There is established correlation between suicide/self-harm and mental health issues (particularly anxiety and substance abuse without remission) and previous suicide attempts in countries with high income, where suicide often occurs during crises. Factors contributing to suicide/self-harm include grief, anxiety, discrimination, broken relationships, financial problems, severe pain and illness, disability, conflict, and other human rights violations. The problem with suicide/self-harm is that people think of suicide/self-harm as not seeking help. Suicide/self-harm or attempts to do so have a devastating impact on families, friends, colleagues, and the community as a whole.



**Notes on the Translation:**

*   **"Ƙunar-baƙin-wake/kisan-kai"**: I've consistently translated this as "suicide/self-harm" to reflect the nuance of the Hausa term, which encompasses both completed suicides and attempts.
*   I've tried to maintain a formal and informative tone, appropriate for the subject matter.
*   I've included explanations where necessary to ensure clarity for an English-speaking audience.

translations.source: ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai: Adadin faruwarsa a duniya Rahoton faruwar ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai a 2019 a duniya Gwagwarmayar Rayuwa: jagora wajen kawar da yin ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai a ƙasashe Kawarwa: Akwai matakai da dama da za a iya ɗauka a matakin ɗaiɗaikun mutane da al'umma da kuma matakin ƙasa wajen kawar da aukuwar ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai da kuma yunƙurin yin hakan. Hanyoyin da Hukumar Lafiya ta bayar ya kamata a yi amfani da su wajen yin maganin ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai a matakin kowace ƙasa, wanda ya haɗa da: hana kaiwa ga hanyoyin ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai mu'amala da kafafen sadarwa domin bayar da cikakken rahoto game da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai koyar da dabarun jure damuwa ga matasa saurin ganowa da kimantawa da kulawa da kuma bibiyar duk wanda yake da ɗabi'un yin ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai . Abu muhimmi wajen daƙile wannan ta'ada shi ne bincike da haɗin gwiwar ɓangarori daban-daban da wayar da kai da bayar da horo da sa-ido a kan hada-hadar kuɗi da kulawa da kuma kimantawa.
translations.reference: Suicide: facts and figures globally Suicide worldwide in 2019 report LIVE LIFE: an implementation guide for suicide prevention in countries Prevention: Much can be done at individual, community and national levels to prevent suicide and suicide attempts. WHO’s LIVE LIFE approach recommends four effective, evidence-based key interventions which should be included in every national response to suicide: limit access to the means of suicide interact with the media for responsible reporting of suicide foster socio-emotional life skills in adolescents early identify, assess, manage and follow up anyone who is affected by suicidal behaviours. The foundational pillars of the interventions are situation analysis, multisectoral collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, financing, surveillance, monitoring and evaluation.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Suicide or Self-Harm: The Extent of its Occurrence Worldwide**

**Report on the occurrence of suicide or self-harm globally in 2019**

**Striving for Life: A Guide to Preventing Suicide in Countries**

**Prevention:** There are several steps that can be taken at the individual, community, and national levels to prevent the occurrence of suicide or self-harm and to strive to do so. The methods provided by the World Health Organization (WHO) should be used in treating suicide or self-harm at the level of each country, which includes:

*   Preventing access to methods of suicide or self-harm
*   Engaging with media to provide accurate reporting on suicide or self-harm
*   Teaching coping skills to young people
*   Rapidly identifying, assessing, and caring for, and following up with anyone exhibiting behaviors indicative of suicide or self-harm.

A crucial aspect of preventing this phenomenon is investigation and collaboration between different sectors, raising awareness, and providing training and focus on coordinating funding, care, and assessment.



**Notes on the Translation:**

*   I've tried to maintain the formal tone of the original Hausa text.
*   "Ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai" is a phrase that encompasses both suicide and self-harm. I've used "suicide or self-harm" to reflect this.
*   I've included the WHO reference to clarify the source of the recommended methods.

translations.source: Yunƙurin kawar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai na buƙatar haɗin-kai da haɗin gwiwa a tsakanin ɓangarori, waɗanda suka haɗa da ɓangaren lafiya da ilimi da ƙwadago da noma da kasuwanci da shari'a da doka da tsaro da siyasa da kuma kafafen watsa labarai. Ba kasafai ake kawar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai yadda ya kamata ba saboda damuwa da rashin wayar da kai, wanda shi ne babban ƙalubalen ɓangaren lafiya. Su ma al'ummomi suna da rawar da za su taka wajen kawar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai. Bayanan Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya na yi wa al'ummomi jagoranci sun bayyana yadda al'ummomi za su gano abubuwan da suka fi muhimmanci wajen aiwatar da aiwatar da su bisa ga kowace al'umma. Bayanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: An ba wa ƙoƙarin gaggauta kawar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai matuƙar fifiko. Raguwar yawan aukuwar ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kisan-kai a ƙasashe alama ce ta ayyukan Hukumar Ci-gaba ta Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya (alama ce kawai a ɓabgaren lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa) da Shirye-shiryen Ayyuka na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya da kuma Shirin Samar da Wadatacciyar Lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa na 2013-2030. Rahoton Hukmar Lafiya ta Duniya a kan ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai na farko ya nuna cewa, kawar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai: rahoton da aka buga a 2014 da manufar ƙara wayar da kai a kan muhimmancin inganta lafiyar al'umma wajen daƙile aukuwar ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai ko kuma yunƙurin yin hakan da kuma da ba wa kawar da aukuwar ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai fifiko tamkar na kula da lafiyar al'umma. An ƙarfafar gwiyoyin gwamnatoci da su aiwatar da shirin Hukumar Lafiya na Rayuwa yadda ya kamata ta hanyar ɗaukar matakan kawar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai da kuma samar da dabarun kawar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai na ƙasa, ta hanyar dabarun kula da lafiya daban-daban. Ana ƙarfafar shugabannni da masu sa-ido a kan kawar da ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai ta hanyar buga rahotanni game da dabarun daƙile ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai, ta hanyar bayyana ci-gaban da aka samu da misalai da kuma alamomi. Sakamakon gamsuwa da muhimmancin da ke tattare da da bayanai da ƙididdiga wajen yanke shawara,Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta buga rahoto aukuwar ƙunar-baƙin-wake ko kashe-kai a duniya a 2019: Hashen Lafiya na Duniya.
translations.reference: Suicide prevention efforts require coordination and collaboration among multiple sectors, including health, education, labour, agriculture, business, justice, law, defence, politics and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed because of stigma and lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The WHO community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying priorities and implementing activities according to local contexts. WHO response: The urgency to act to prevent suicides has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels. The reduction of suicide rates in countries is an indicator in the UN Sustainable Development Goals (the only indicator for mental health), WHO’s General Programme of Work and WHO’s Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The first WHO world suicide report, Preventing suicide: a global imperative, published in 2014, aimed to increase awareness of the public health significance of suicide and suicide attempts and make suicide prevention a public health priority. Governments are encouraged to implement WHO’s LIVE LIFE package of effective and evidence-based key interventions for suicide prevention, and to develop or strengthen comprehensive national suicide prevention strategies in a multisectoral public health approach. National leadership and coordination on suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of National suicide prevention strategies: progress, examples and indicators. Recognizing that data and statistics are necessary for advocacy and decision-making, WHO published Suicide worldwide in 2019: Global Health Estimates.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"Efforts to eliminate suicide or self-harm require collaboration and partnership among various sectors, including health, education, development, agriculture, commerce, justice, law, security, politics, and the media. Suicide or self-harm are not always adequately addressed due to stigma and lack of awareness, which is a major challenge for the health sector. Communities also have a role to play in eliminating suicide or self-harm. World Health Organization (WHO) data guides communities on how to identify the most important issues for implementation according to each community. The World Health Organization states: Efforts to eliminate suicide or self-harm should be given priority. The prevalence of suicide or self-harm in countries is an indicator of the work of the United Nations Development Programme (specifically in the area of mental health), the World Health Organization's Action Plans, and the Mental Health Action Plan 2013-2030. The World Health Organization's first report on suicide or self-harm showed that eliminating suicide or self-harm: the report published in 2014 aimed to increase awareness of the importance of improving public health in preventing the occurrence of suicide or self-harm or attempting to do so, and to prioritize eliminating suicide or self-harm as if it were a matter of public health. Governments are encouraged to implement the WHO's Mental Health Action Plan properly by taking measures to eliminate suicide or self-harm and developing national strategies for eliminating suicide or self-harm through various health interventions. Leaders and stakeholders are encouraged to address suicide or self-harm by publishing reports on strategies for preventing suicide or self-harm, highlighting progress, examples, and indicators. Recognizing the value and importance of data and statistics in decision-making, the World Health Organization published a report on the global occurrence of suicide or self-harm in 2019: The World Health Report."



I hope this translation is helpful!

translations.reference: WHO’s vision is a world where death by suicide is no longer a leading cause of death and where people who are attempting or thinking about suicide feel comfortable seeking help which is provided capably and respectfully to all.

translations.source: Ci-gaba Mai Ɗorewa ƙarin bayani: Ci-gaba mai ɗorewa kalmomin fannu ne da ke bayyana dokoki da shirue-shirye da saka hannun jari da zai kai ga samun riba a yanzu ba tare da baddala muhalli ko tsarin zamantakewa ko lafiya a nan gaba ba. Ana bayyana waɗannan dokoki da koraye, saboda suna mayar da hankali ne wajen taƙaita tasirin ci-gaba a kan muhalli. Sai dai an amfana da ci-gaba mai ɗorewa a ko'ina a duniya, wanda ya haɗa da kowane ɓangare na lafiya da jin daɗin ɗan'adam, kama daga raguwar yawan gurɓatar yanayi da cututtuka masu nasaba da muhalli da ingantuwar lafiyar da raguwar wahalhalu. A yayin da barazanar sauyawar yanayi ke da muhimmanci, dabarun samun nasara ta kowane ɓangare wajen magancewa da inganta lafiya da rage kashe kuɗi na da matuƙar muhimmanci ga masu ruwa da tsaki. Alal misali, Shirin Lafiya na 'greener health care operations' zai samar da alfanu ga mara lafiya da ma'aikatan lafiya, da kuma rage amfani da makamashi, wanda shi kuma sauƙaƙa barazanar sauyin yanayi da kuma da kuma rage kashe kuɗi na tsawon lokaci. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta ƙudiri aniyar tabbatar da ci-gaba mai ɗorewa a duk wasu ayyuka da take aiwatarwa, domin taimakawa wajen kare mutane masu zuwa nan gaba daga barazanar lafiya da ake fuskanta a yanzu. Tasiri: Da dama daga cikin dabarun shirin ci-gaba mai ɗorewa za su matuƙar taimakawa wajen samuwar lafiya da sauyin yanayi da muhalli, sannan kuma za a ga kusan alfaninsu nan take. Alal misali, za a iya cim ma nasarar da dama daga cikin manufofin lafiya da na ci-gaba a lokaci guda, ta hanyar shawo kan matsalar gurɓatar iska, wadda ita ta yi silar kimanin mace-mace milyan 7 duk shekara. Abubuwan da suke cikin gurɓatacciyar iska sun haɗa da abubuwa masu ƙarfi da ka iya ɗumama yanayi, musamman ma kabon da ozon da kuma mesan, waɗanda suke cutar da lafiya, amma kuma ba su fiye daɗewa ba.
translations.reference: Sustainable development Overview: Sustainable development is a broad term to describe policies, projects and investments that provide benefits today without sacrificing environmental, social and personal health in the future. These policies are often described as green because they focus on limiting the impact of development on the environment. However, the benefits of sustainable development are also felt across a wide cross section of human health and well-being, including reductions in pollution- and environment-related disease, improved health outcomes and decreased stress. As the threats of climate change become increasingly important, win-win strategies for mitigation, health improvement and cost savings offer a range of advantages for various stakeholders. For example, greener health care operations can generate patient and worker health benefits while also saving energy, mitigating climate risks and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organization is committed to pursuing sustainable development in all its work to help protect the people of tomorrow from the health growing health risks of today. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can offer significant wins for health, climate and the environment, and the benefits can be seen almost immediately. For example, many health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by tackling air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths annually. Polluting emissions include powerful short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—particularly black carbon, ozone and methane—which are very harmful to health but have only brief atmospheric lifetimes.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Sustainable Development: Further Information**

Sustainable development is a term encompassing policies, programs, and investments that aim to achieve benefits now without compromising the environment, social systems, or health in the future. These policies and guidelines focus on minimizing the impact of development on the environment. Sustainable development is being implemented worldwide, incorporating every aspect of human well-being, from reducing pollution and environmentally related diseases to improving health and alleviating suffering. As the threat of climate change looms large, strategies for successful action involving all sectors to address and improve health and reduce costs are critically important for stakeholders. For example, a 'greener health care operations' program would provide benefits to patients and healthcare workers, as well as reduce energy consumption, thereby mitigating the threat of climate change and reducing long-term costs. The World Health Organization is committed to ensuring sustainable development in all its operations, to help protect future generations from the health threats currently being faced.

**Impact:**

Many of the strategies within sustainable development programs will help achieve health, climate change, and environmental goals, and their benefits will be seen almost immediately. For instance, several health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by tackling air pollution, which causes an estimated 7 million deaths each year. The components of polluted air include potent substances that can warm the climate, particularly carbon and ozone, which harm health but are not persistent.

translations.source: Dokokin sufuri da zuba hannun jarin da ka iya tsaftace hanyoyin sufurin jama'a da kuma tafiyar ƙasa da tuƙa keke na da matuƙar tasiri a kan ingancin iska. Wannan zai taƙaita barazana iri-iri ga lafiya, wanda ya haɗa da shayenwar ɓarin jiki da ciwon zuciya da cututtukan hunhu da kuma kansa. Haka kuma za su iya rage kimanin mace-mace miliyan 1.25 sanadiyyar samun rauni a duk shekara, yayin da kuma za su inganta kaiwa ga kula da lafiya, musamman a wuraren da suke da yawan jama'a. Dokokin Shirin Ci-gaba Mai Ɗorewa na samar da gidaje zai ƙara rage ƙura ta hanyar gine-gine da amfani da filaye da zaɓar kayan gini da tsari da kuma samar da gine-gine na yalwar iska da kuma makamashi. Matakin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: Aikace-aikacen Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya na tafiya ne bisa manufar Shirin Ci-gaba Mai Ɗorewa, musamman ma manufar ta 3: '' Tabbatar da lafiyar mutane da ƙaruwar walwalar kowa da kowa''. Manufofin na shelanta shiye-shirye da dokoki a hukumar, waɗanda suka haɗa da dokokin gina gidaje da gina birane da tsarin sufuri da samar da kulawa da lafiya. Alal misali, dokokin gina gidaje da kula lafiya na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya da aka fara bugawa a 2018, sun yi ƙarin haske a kan ƙaruwar tasirin yanayin gidaje a kan lafiyar mutum a yayin da birane suka bunƙasa, inda sauyin yanayi da yawan jama'a ke sauyawa. Dokokin sun ba wa mambobin ƙasashe da abokan hulɗa da sauran jama'a shawarwari da ke da nasaba da cunkoso da haɗarin cikin gida da kuma samar da gidaje ga mutanen da ke da tawaya. Shi ma Shirin Lafiya a Birane ya mayar da hanakali ne wajen samar da cikakkiyar lafiya a birane, ta hanyar gaggauta ɗaukar matakai a kan guɓacewar iska a birane da kuma abubuwan da ke haifar da ɗumamar yanayi, domin ceto rayuka masu matsalar lafiya da muhalli da ci-gaba mai ɗorewa. Haka kuma Hukumar Lafiya ta yi haɗin gwiwa da Gamayyar ƙungiyar Kula da Sauyin Yanayi da Iska Mai kyau wajen wayar da kai a kan sheƙar numfashi domin rayuwa, wanda manufarsa ita ce kawo ƙarshen gurɓacewar iska da rage mace-macen mutane miliyan 7 da ke haifarwa a duk shekara.
translations.reference: Transport policies and investments that favour clean public transport, along with walking and cycling, can have a dramatic impact on air quality. This can reduce multiple health risks at the same time, including stroke, heart attack, lung disease and some cancers. They can also reduce the estimated 1.25 million deaths annually from traffic injury, while also improving access to health services, particularly in densely populated areas. Sustainable development housing policies can further reduce emissions through considerations such as building siting and land use, choices of construction materials, design features and ventilation and energy. WHO response: WHO’s work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3: “Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.” The Goals inform projects and policies across the Organization, including guidelines on housing, urban development, transportation systems and health care delivery. For example, the WHO Housing and health guidelines, first published in 2018, highlight the increasing impact of housing conditions on human health in light of urban growth, climate and demographic changes. The guidelines provide Member States, partners and the public with evidence-based recommendations relevant to inadequate living space (crowding), low and high indoor temperatures, injury hazards in the home, and accessibility of housing for people with functional impairments. The Urban Health Initiative (UHI) also focuses on ways for cities to enable good health by catalysing effective action on urban air pollution and short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—saving lives by linking health, environment and sustainable development. WHO also collaborates with the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) on the Breathe Life campaign, which aims to cut air pollution and reduce the 7 million premature deaths annually that it causes.

translations.source: Tinjere ƙarin bayani: Tinjere cuta ce da ake ɗauka yayin saduwar namiji da mace sakamakon ƙwayar cuta, wadda ke haifar da rashin lafiya da kuma mutuwa, amma ana iya warkewa. Tinjere cuta ce da ke yaxuwa a yayin jima'i, inda ake ɗaukar cuta kuma take yaɗuwa ko dai ta hanyar jin ko kuma daga uwa mai ciki zuwa jaririn da ke cikinta. Yaɗuwar cutar tinjere daga uwa zuwa jariri yawanci yana kassara jaririn, musamman idan ba a gano ba ko kuma an ɗauki mataki a farkon juna-biyun. Idan ba a yi maganin yaɗuwar tinjere daga uwa zuwa jariri ko ba a yi magani da wuri ba ko ma ba a yi maganinta da fenisilin, tana haifar da matsala yayin haihuwa da kimanin kaso 50-80, ya danganta da matakin cutar ta tinjere. Wannan sakamako yawanci yakan tsananta, sannan kuma ya haɗa da jariran da ke mutuwa yayin haihuwa (wanda ya fi yawa) da mutuwa bayan haihuwa da kafin balaga da rashin nauyin jariri da kuma jariran da suka ɗauki cuta daga uwa. Za a iya maganin cutar tinjere ta hanyar yin taka-tsantsan wajen yin jima'i, wanda ya haɗa da yawan yin amfani da kwaroron roba. Mutane da dama da suka kamu da cutar tinjere ba su ganin wasu alamu. Tabbatar da cuta ta hanyar yin gwaji da kuma yin magana zai hana yaɗuwarta da kuma haifar da matsala a yayin haihuwa, wanda ya haɗa da nau'in tinjere da jariri ke ɗauka daga uwa. Za a iya tabbatarwa ta hanyar yawaita yin gwaje-gwaje da za su bayar da sakamako a ƙasa da minti 20, domin yin magani nan take.
translations.reference: Syphilis Overview: Syphilis is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by Treponema pallidum which results in substantial morbidity and mortality, and it is curable. Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact with the infectious lesions, via blood transfusion or from a pregnant woman to her fetus. Mother-to-child transmission of syphilis is usually devastating to the fetus in cases that were not detected or treated sufficiently early in pregnancy. Maternal syphilis, when untreated, treated late or not treated with penicillin, results in adverse birth outcomes (ABOs) estimated in 50−80% of cases, depending on the stage of syphilis. These ABOs are often severe and include stillbirth (most commonly), neonatal death, prematurity, low birth weight, and congenitally infected infants. Syphilis can be prevented through safer sex practices including the correct and consistent use of condoms. Many people with syphilis do not have any symptoms or have only minor symptoms that go unnoticed. Identifying infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes including congenital syphilis. Screening can be done using rapid tests that can provide results in less than 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Additional Information: Syphilis**

Syphilis is an infection contracted during sexual intercourse with an infected person, caused by a pathogen that leads to illness and potentially death, but it is treatable. Syphilis is a sexually transmitted infection, where the infection is contracted and spread either through contact or from a pregnant woman to her unborn child. The transmission of syphilis from mother to child often affects the child, especially if it is not detected or if no action is taken early on. If the mother-to-child transmission of syphilis is not treated promptly or even if it is not treated with penicillin, it causes complications during childbirth with an estimated 50-80% rate, depending on the stage of the syphilis infection. This outcome is usually severe and includes babies dying during childbirth (which is more common), deaths after childbirth and before adulthood, low birth weight, and babies who contract the infection from their mother.

Syphilis can be prevented by practicing safe sex, which includes consistent use of condoms. Many people who contract syphilis do not experience any symptoms. Confirming the infection through testing and treatment will prevent its spread and complications during childbirth, including the type of syphilis the child is contracting from the mother. Confirmation can be achieved through tests that provide results in under 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment.

translations.source: Ida ba a ɗauKi mataki, tinjere kan ɗauki shekaru a jikin mutum, ta hanyar kaiwa matakai daban-daban. Ana maganin cutar tinjere ta hanyar yin allurar fenicilin sau 1 zuwa 3, ya danganta da matakin cutar. Fenicilin ne kawai yake maganin tinjeren da ake ɗauka daga uwa zuwa yaro. Yin magani da fenicilin ya samu tsaiko sakamakon rashin fenicilin benzatayin na tsawon lokaci a ƙasashe da dama, waɗanda suka haɗa da ƙasashe masu yawa da ƙaranci da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga. Alamomi: Mutane da dama da ke fama da cutar tinjere ba su ganin wasu alamomi. Cutar tinjeren da ba yi mata magani ba takan ɗauki shekaru a jikin mutum, inda take xaukar matakai daban-daban. Cutar tana yaxuwa ta hanyar jima'i a mataki farko, wato kusan shekar 2 bayan kawuwa da cutar. A matakin farko, wanda yake ɗaukar kwana 21, inda ita kaɗai kuma mara zafi, wurin kan kawashe yawanci a farji ko zakari ko kuma takashi (ko kuma gaba ɗaya), sannan kuma za a iya kasa gane ta. Ciwon ka iya warkewa ba tare da magani ba a cikin mako 2 zuwa 10. Idan ba a yi magani ba, cutar kan ƙara gaba zuwa wani mataki.
translations.reference: When untreated, syphilis lasts many years and goes through different stages. Treatment for syphilis is done with injectable penicillin benzathine, with 1 to 3 doses depending on the stage of the disease. Only penicillin can prevent congenital syphilis. Treatment with penicillin has been hampered by periodic and prolonged shortages of benzathine penicillin in numerous countries, including high-, low- and middle-income countries. Symptoms: Many people with syphilis do not present symptoms or do not perceive them. Untreated, syphilis lasts many years and is characterized into different stages. Sexual transmission typically occurs during early stages, i.e., up to 2 years after infection. During the initial phase (primary syphilis), which lasts on average 21 days, a solitary, painless, usually hard and round sore (chancre) appears at the site of inoculation (contact), often in the vagina, penis or anus (but it may also be extra-genital) and may go unnoticed. The chancre heals with or without treatment within 3 to 10 weeks. If untreated, the disease progresses to the secondary stage.
