"If no action is taken, syphilis can progress through the body over years, through different stages. Syphilis is treated with penicillin injections, typically 1 to 3 doses, depending on the stage of the disease. Penicillin is the only medication that effectively treats the syphilis transmitted from mother to child. Treatment with penicillin has become more difficult due to the lack of benzathine penicillin for an extended period in many countries, including low, middle, and high-income nations.

Symptoms: Many people with syphilis do not experience any symptoms. Untreated syphilis can persist in the body for years, progressing through different stages. The disease initially spreads through sexual contact, typically around 2 years after infection. In the primary stage, which lasts about 21 days, it is often painless, with a sore typically appearing on the genitals, anus, or mouth (or elsewhere), and may be difficult to detect. The infection can heal without treatment within 2 to 10 weeks. If left untreated, the disease progresses to a later stage."

translations.source: Cutar tinjere nau'i na biyu na siffantauwa ne da fitowar ƙuraje, waɗanda suka bambanta, sannan su haifar da cututtuka masu yaɗuwa ko marasa yaɗuwa, amma yawanci suna shafar tafin hannu da na ƙafa. ƙurajen ba masu ƙaƙayi ba ne, inda za a iya watsi da su. A sassan jikin da suke da lema ko danshi kamar takashi da leɓe, wani farin abu ko ruwan ƙasa kan fito a wurin da ciwon asali yake. Alamomin nau'in tinjere na biyu kan iya ɓacewa, ba tare da magani ba. Idan ba a ɗauki mataki ba, yanayin iya ci gaba. Irin wannan yanayi na cutar tinjere ba shi da wasu alamomi, sannan maganinsa ya bambanta da na lokacin da ya fara. Idan aka ɗauki shekaru ba tare da yin magani ba, yakan haifar da matsalar ƙwaƙwalwa da shanyewar ɓarin jiki da sauransu. Magani: Maganin cutar tinjere ya danganta ne da tarihin mutum da kallon sa a zahiri da gwaji da kuma ɗaukar hoto a wasu lokutan, domin kuwa alamomin ba ɗaya ba ne ko muma ba a gane su. Gano cuta ta hanyar gwaji da kuma yin magani zai hana yaɗuwar cutar da kuma haifar da mutuwar jariri a yayin haihuwa, wanda ya haɗa da nau'in tinjeren da ake ɗauka a yayin haihuwa. Gwajin cutar tinjere ya qunshi gano wurin kai-tsaye ko kuma ta hanyar kaikaice, wadda ta haɗa da ɗaukar jini.
translations.reference: Secondary syphilis is characterized by a rash that can vary widely and mimic other infectious or non-infectious conditions, but characteristically affects the palms and soles. The rash is non-itchy and can be minimal enough to be overlooked. In warm and moist areas of the body, such as the anus and labia, large white or grey raised lesions may develop at the location of the primary chancre (condyloma lata). The symptoms and signs of secondary syphilis spontaneously resolve, even without treatment. Without treatment, the latent stage starts. Latent syphilis presents no clinical symptoms or signs and the treatment is different from the early phase. After years or decades without treatment, neurological and cardiovascular diseases among others may occur. Treatment: Syphilis diagnosis is based on the person’s history, physical examination, laboratory testing and sometimes radiology, as symptoms are not common or noticeable. Identifying asymptomatic infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes, including congenital syphilis. Laboratory tests for syphilis include direct detection of T. pallidum or indirect methods such as serology.

"This is a type 2 fungal infection characterized by the emergence of lesions, which vary, and can cause contagious or non-contagious diseases, but commonly affect the hands and feet. These lesions are not always obvious and can be overlooked. In areas of the body with moisture or fluids, such as the nails and lips, a white substance or pus may appear at the site of the original infection. Symptoms of type 2 fungal infection can sometimes disappear without treatment. If no action is taken, the condition can progress. This type of fungal infection may not have obvious symptoms, and its presentation can differ from when it initially started. If left untreated for years, it can lead to neurological problems, disfigurement of the body, and other complications.

Treatment: Treatment for fungal infection depends on the patient's history, physical examination, and testing, and sometimes imaging, as the symptoms are not always the same or easily recognizable. Diagnosing the infection through testing and treatment will prevent the spread of the infection and prevent infant mortality during childbirth, including congenital fungal infections. Fungal infection testing involves direct detection or microscopic examination, which includes blood tests."

translations.source: Yawan dubawa da kuma gwaji shi ma ka iya ba da sakamako a ƙasa da minti 20, wanda hakan kan taimaka wajen yin magani nan take a farkon cutar. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince da yin maganin tinjere ta amfani da allurar fenicilin, ta hanyar yin allura biyu zuwa 3, ya danganta da matakin cutar. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta ƙarin bayani game da matakan magance cutar tinjere, inda ta fayyace wanda za a duba da kuma maganin tinjere a yayin da ake da juna biyu. Cutar tinjeren da ake ɗauka yayin haihuwa A yanzu babu wani gwaji na cutar tinjere da ake ɗauka a yayin haihuwa. Duk rayayyun jarirai da waɗanda suka mutu yayin haihuwa sakamakon cutar tinjere ya kamata duba su ko suna da cutar da ake ɗauka yayin haihuwa. Ya kamata a duba tare da gwajin jariran da suke raye a yayin da aka haife su domin fayyace irin maganin da suke buƙata. A yayin da aka tabbatar da cutar, ya kamata duk bayan wata 3 a riƙa ɗaukar jini , sannan a uwar za ta ci gaba da shan magunguna a lokacin da take da juna har sai cutar ta tafi.
translations.reference: Rapid screening and diagnostic tests are also available and can provide results in less than 20 minutes, facilitating immediate treatment initiation. WHO recommends syphilis to be treated with injectable penicillin (benzathine penicillin G) with 1 or 3 doses, depending on the stage of the disease. WHO has detailed treatment guidelines for syphilis, and one specifically for the screening and treatment of syphilis in pregnancy. Congenital syphilis There are currently no diagnostic tests for congenital syphilis. All live or stillborn infants of women with syphilis should be examined for evidence of congenital syphilis. Live-born infants should be examined and tested at birth to define treatment requirement. When indicated, also at monthly intervals for 3 months until the infant’s serology are, and remain, negative, as maternal antibodies can be passively transmitted to the baby during pregnancy.

"The number of examinations and tests can also provide results in less than 20 minutes, which helps in treating the disease immediately at the onset. The World Health Organization has approved treating sepsis with penicillin injections, through two to three injections, depending on the severity of the infection. The World Health Organization has provided additional information on the stages of managing sepsis, clarifying who should be examined and treated for sepsis when there is a mutual agreement. Congenital sepsis – Currently, there is no test for congenital sepsis. All live babies and those who died during childbirth as a result of sepsis should be examined to see if they have congenital sepsis. They should be examined along with tests for babies who are alive at birth to determine the type of treatment they need. Once the disease is confirmed, blood should be taken every three months, and the mother should continue taking medication during pregnancy until the infection is gone."

translations.source: Cutar dauɗar ciki da ta cushewar ciki ƙarin bayani: cutar dauɗar ciki na samuwa ne sakamakon abubuwa guda biyu mabambanta: cushewar ciki da cushewar hanji. Cushewar hanji cuta ce ta dauɗar ciki, wadda take samuwa a laokacin da mutum ya ci zallar naman alade ko wanda ba a dafa ko kuma naman aladen da ke da cuta. Cutar cushewar ciki ba ta da wani tasiri a kan lafiya. Sai dai kashin mai dauɗar ciki wanda ba a tsafatace ba ka iya gurɓata muhalli. A yayin da ƙwayaƙwan mcijin ciki ba sa yi wa alade komai, T. solium eggs ka iya illata ɗan'adam, idan mutum bai haɗiyu ba ( ta hanyar baki ko gurɓataccen abinci ko ruwan sha). Rashin narkewar tsutsar jiki na haifar da cuta, inda za samu a cuta a sassan jiki kamar tsokar jiki da fata da idanu da kuma ƙwaƙwalwa, wanda hakan ka iya cutar da lafiya. A yayin da aka samu kumburi a ƙwaƙwalwa, ana kiran wannan yanayi da kumburin ƙwaƙwalwa,. Alamomi: Dauɗar ciki yawanci tana siffantuwa ne da rashin wasu tsayayyun alamomi waɗanda za a iya dangantawa da cutar hanji (ciwon ciki da jin amai da gudawa da sauransu). Waɗannan alamomi za su iya ci gaba har sai tsutsar ta mutu sakamakon shan magani, ko kuma ta ci gaba da rayuwa tsawon shekaru.
translations.reference: Taeniasis and cysticercosis Overview: Infection with the tapeworm Taenia solium can result in two distinct conditions: taeniasis and cysticercosis. Taeniasis is the intestinal infection with the tapeworm and it occurs when a person eats raw or undercooked, infected pork. Taeniasis has no major impact on human health. However, tapeworm eggs pass in the faeces of the tapeworm carrier and in the absence of a proper sanitation may contaminate the environment. While tapeworm eggs are infective for pigs, T. solium eggs may also infect humans if they are ingested by a person (via the fecal-oral route, or by ingesting contaminated food or water). Ingestion of tapeworm eggs causes infection with the larval parasite in the tissues such as muscles, skin, eyes and the central nervous system (human cysticercosis), with possible devastating effects on health. When cysts develop in the brain, the condition is referred to as neurocysticercosis. Symptoms: Taeniasis is usually characterized by mild and non-specific symptoms associated to an intestinal infection (abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhoea, etc.). These symptoms may continue until the tapeworm dies following treatment, otherwise it may live for years.

**Tapeworm Infection and Intestinal Flukes: Additional Information**

Tapeworm infection occurs as a result of two distinct factors: intestinal flukes and tapeworm. Intestinal fluke is a type of tapeworm infection that occurs when a person consumes raw or undercooked pork, or pork that is infected. Intestinal fluke has no effect on health. However, the feces of a tapeworm carrier that is not properly treated can contaminate the environment. While the larval stages of the fluke do not affect the pig, *T. solium* eggs can affect humans if they are ingested (through the mouth, contaminated food, or drinking water). The lack of immune response causes infection, which can occur in various parts of the body such as the intestines, skin, eyes, and brain, which can harm health. When swelling occurs in the brain, this condition is called cerebral edema (brain swelling).

**Symptoms:** Tapeworm infection is usually characterized by a lack of specific symptoms that can be attributed to tapeworm (abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhea, etc.). These symptoms can persist until the parasite dies as a result of medication, or they can continue to live for years.

**Key improvements and explanations of choices:**

*   **"Cutar dauɗar ciki"** is consistently translated as "Tapeworm infection."
*   **"Cushewar ciki"** is translated as "Intestinal Flukes" to be more precise.
*   **"Cushewar hanji"** is translated as "tapeworm" to clarify the type of infection.
*   I've added explanations where necessary to ensure clarity for an English-speaking audience (e.g., explaining "cerebral edema").
*   I've tried to maintain a formal and informative tone appropriate for a medical or informational document.
*   I've used more natural English phrasing while staying true to the original meaning.
*   I've included the scientific name *T. solium* for accuracy.

translations.source: Game da cutar cushewar ciki, lokacin yin ƙyanƙyansa kan bambanta, sannan kuma masu fama da matsalar za su ci gaba da kasancewa ba tare da wata alama ba tsawon shekaru. A wasu wuraren da suka fama da annoba, waɗanda suka kamu ka iya siffantuwa da wasu abubuwa da za a iya gani (abun da za iya taɓawa) a ƙasan fata. Kumburin ƙwaƙwalwa na bayyan ne da alamomi iri-iri, ya danganta ga da adadi da girma da mataki da kuma wurin da cutar ta fito da kuma yadda garkuwar jiki ta bayar da kariya da ƙwayar halittar mai cutar, sai dai za ta iya kasancewa ba ta nuna wata alama ba. Alamomin wannan cuta za su iya kasncewa matsanancin ciwo kai da makanta da farfaɗiya da taruwar ruwa a ƙwaƙwalwa da zazzaɓi da cutar mantuwa da kum sauran alamomin da raunin ya fito a ƙwaƙwalwa ya haifar. Magani: Maganin dauɗar ciki na da muhimmanci wajen hana aukuwar cutar kumburi da jan ruwan ƙwaƙwalwa a matasayin wata hanya ta hana ko tsakayar da hana yaɗuwar ƙwayoyin cuta. Za a iya yin magani dangane da yanayin mutane daban-daban ko ta yawan shan magani bisa ga yanayi wuri da kuma hanyar dakatar da yaɗuwar cutar da ka yi amfani da ita. za a iya maganin cutar dauɗar ciki ta hanyar amfani da maganin macijin ciki. Game da matsalar kumburi da jan ruwan ƙwaƙwalwa, fashewar ruwan kan iya yi zafi, ana buƙatar magani cuta na musamman, wanda ka iya ɗaukar matakai masu tsayi da kuma shan maganin macijin ciki ko allura mai yawa da kuma sauran magungunan farfaɗiya da kuma yuwuwar yin aiki. Yawan magani da tsawon lokacin da za a ɗauka ana sha ya danganta da adadi da girma da wuri da kuma matakin kumburin da zafin kumburin da tsininsa da tsananinsa da kuma alamominsa.
translations.reference: For cysticercosis, the incubation period is variable, and infected people may remain asymptomatic for years. In some endemic regions, infected people may develop visible or palpable nodules (a small solid bump or node which is solid that can be detected by touch) beneath the skin. Neurocysticercosis is associated with a variety of signs and symptoms depending on the number, size, stage and location of the pathological changes as well as the host’s immune response and the parasite’s genotype, but it can also be clinically asymptomatic. Symptoms may include chronic headaches, blindness, seizures (epilepsy if they are recurrent), hydrocephalus, meningitis, dementia and symptoms caused by lesions occupying spaces of the central nervous system. Treatment: The treatment of taeniasis by Taenia solium is important to prevent neurocysticercosis and as a tool to assist in controlling or stopping the parasite transmission cycle. The treatment can be done on an individual bases, or as Mass Drug Administration depending on the local circumstances and the control approaches being implemented. Taeniasis can be treated using anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). In neurocysticercosis, since the destruction of cysts may lead to an inflammatory response, specialised treatment of active disease is required and may include long courses with high doses of anthelmintic, as well as supporting therapy with corticosteroids and/or anti-epileptic drugs, and possibly surgery. The dosage and the duration of treatment can vary greatly and depend mainly on the number, size, location and developmental stage of the cysts, their surrounding inflammatory edema, acuteness and severity of clinical symptoms or signs.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Dealing with Brain Abscesses**

The onset of a brain abscess can vary, and those suffering from the condition may remain symptom-free for years. In areas heavily affected by the disease, infected individuals may exhibit visible (tangible) lesions under the skin.

The presentation of a brain abscess can vary greatly, depending on the number, size, stage, and location of the infection, as well as the body's defense mechanisms and the characteristics of the pathogen. However, it can also be asymptomatic. Symptoms of this disease can include severe headaches, nausea, vomiting, fluid buildup in the brain, fever, memory loss, and other symptoms resulting from brain damage.

**Treatment:** Treatment for internal abscesses is crucial in preventing the development of brain swelling and fluid buildup, serving as a way to prevent or halt the spread of the infection. Treatment can be tailored to individual patient conditions or adjusted based on the environment and the method used to stop the spread of the infection. Internal abscesses can be treated with internal medicine. Regarding the problem of brain swelling and fluid buildup, the fluid discharge can be painful and requires specific treatment, which may involve lengthy procedures, multiple doses of internal medicine or injections, other supportive medications, and potentially surgery. The dosage of medication and the duration of treatment depend on the number, size, location, and stage of the swelling, the severity of the swelling, its inflammation, and its symptoms.

translations.source: Cutar tetanos ƙarin bayan: Ttetanos babbar cuta ce da ake ɗauka daga wasu ƙwayoyin halitta da ke rayuwa a cikin ƙasa da yawu da kuma taki. ƙwayar halittar na shiga jikin mutum ne ta hanyar wani yanka mai zurfi ko ciwo ko ƙonuwa da yake shafar ƙwaƙwalwa. Cutar kan haifar da ciwo a jiki, musamman muƙamuƙi da wuya, wanda aka fi sani da lockjaw. Kowane rukuni na mutane ka iya kamuwa da cutar tetanos, to amma cutar ta fi yawa da tsanani ga jariari da iyayensu, a yayin da uwar ba ta samu kari daga cutar ba ta hanyar allura,. Cutar tetanos da ta kama mai juna biya ko kuma mako 6 kafin haihuwa ana kiran ta da cutar tetano ta uwa, yayin da wadda yaro ya kamu da ita a lokacin da yake da kawana 28 ana kiran ta da tetanos ta lokacin haihuwa. Cutar tetanos na ɗaya daga cikin manyan cututtuka da ke addabar sassa daban-daban na duniya, musamman ƙasashe mara arziki, inda ba a samun wadatar kulawa da garkuwr jiki da yawan al'adun rashin zabta a yayin haihuwa. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta yi sashe a 2018 cewa (wadda ita ce shekara mafi ƙaranci da aka yi hasashen), kimanin jarirai 25,000 ne ke mutuwa bayan haihuwarsu sakamakon cutar tetanos, inda aka samu raguwar kaso 88% a 2000. Alamomi: Kusan dukkan matsalar cutar tetabos na furuwa ne ga mutanen da ba taɓa yi musu allura ko manyan da ba su sake yin allura bayan wadda suka yi a baya. Alamomin cutar tetanos kan iya bayyana kowane lokaci daga kwana 3 zuwa 21 da shigar ƙwayar cutar jikin mutum ta hanyar rauni da ya ji .
translations.reference: Tetanus Overview: Tetanus is a serious illness contracted through exposure to the spores of the bacterium, Clostridium tetani, which live in soil, saliva, dust and manure. The bacteria can enter the body through a deep cuts, wounds or burns affecting the nervous system. The infection leads to painful muscle contractions, particularly of the jaw and neck muscle, and is commonly known as “lockjaw”. People of all ages can get tetanus but the disease is particularly common and serious in newborn babies and their mothers when the mother is unprotected from tetanus by the vaccine, tetanus toxoid. Tetanus occurring during pregnancy or within 6 weeks of the end of pregnancy is called maternal tetanus, while tetanus occurring within the first 28 days of life is called neonatal tetanus. The disease remains an important public health problem in many parts of the world, but especially in low-income countries or districts, where immunization coverage is low and unclean birth practices are common. WHO estimates that in 2018 (the latest year for which estimates are available), 25 000 newborns died from neonatal tetanus, 88% reduction from the situation in 2000. Symptoms: Nearly all cases of tetanus occur in people who have never been vaccinated or in adults who have not kept up to date on their booster shots. Signs and symptoms of tetanus appear anytime from 3 to 21 days after tetanus bacteria enter the body through a wound.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Tetanus Additional Information:** Tetanus is a serious disease contracted from natural spores that live in soil, dust, and rust. The spores enter the body through a deep wound, injury, or burn that affects the nerves. The disease can cause stiffness in the body, especially the neck and jaw, commonly known as lockjaw. Any group of people can contract tetanus, but it is more common and severe in newborns and their mothers, especially when the mother has not received protection from the disease through vaccination. Tetanus contracted by a mother within the 6 weeks before delivery is called maternal tetanus, while tetanus contracted by a baby at 28 weeks gestation is called neonatal tetanus. Tetanus is one of the major diseases that plagues different parts of the world, especially poor countries, where there is a lack of adequate care, protection, and many unsafe delivery practices. The World Health Organization reported in 2018 (which was the year with the lowest estimate) that approximately 25,000 newborns die after birth due to tetanus, a 88% reduction from 2000.

**Symptoms:** Almost all cases of tetanus occur in people who have never been vaccinated or adults who have not received a booster shot after their initial vaccination. The symptoms of tetanus can appear at any time from 3 to 21 days after the spores enter the body through a wound.

Alamomin sun ƙunshi: ƙagewar muƙamuƙi da kasa buɗe baki ƙagewar jiki, musamman baya da ciki da tafin hannu da na ƙafa ƙagewar jiki da kuma jin zafi na faruwa ne sakamakon ƙara wahala wajen yin haɗiya suma ciwon kai zazzaɓi da zufa Sauyawar hawan jini ko kuma saurin bugawar zuciya.

translations.source: A tetanos ɗin lokacin haihuwa, alamomin kan haɗa da ƙagewar jiki, wanda yawanci ke gabatar kasa tsotsar nonon jariri da kuma yawan kuka. Ana tabbatar da cutar tetanos dangane da alamu ba tare da buƙatar yin gwaji ba. Cutar tetanos na buƙatar kulawar asibiti, musamman tura zuwa wani asibitin. Mutanen da suka samu sauƙi daga cutar tetanos ba su da garkuwar jiki ta halicci, sannan kuma za su iya sake kamuwa, don haka suna buƙatar riga-kafi. Kariya: Za a iya samun kariya daga cutar tetanos ta hanyar yin riga-kafi da alluaran da ke kashe ta, waɗanda ake amfani da su a lokacin shirin zagayen riga-kafi na duniya da yin ta a yayin da ake zuwa duba lafiyar juna-biyu. Idan mutum ba ya so ya kamu da cutar har abada, hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta bayar da shawarar cewa, mutum ya riƙa yin allura da ke kashe tetanos sau 6 (na fari 3 da kuma riga-kafi) na alluran da kashe tetanos,. Alluran farko 3 a jere ya kamata a yi su, mako 6 da haihuwa, inda daga bisani bayan yin allurar da mako 4. An fi son yin riga-kafin ta 3 a shekara ta biyu bayan haihuwa (wata 12 zuwa 23) a shekara 4 zuwa 7 da kuma shekara 9 zuwa 15. An fi son a yi aƙalla shekara 4 a tsakanin alluran riga-kafin. Akwai allurai iri daban-daban da za a iya amfani da su wajen samun kariya daga cutar tetanos: allurar kashe tetatnos a lokacin yarinta da ta hana kawuwa da cutar tetanos da alluran hana tari.
translations.reference: In neonatal tetanus, symptoms include muscle spasms, which are often preceded by the newborn’s inability to suck or breastfeed, and excessive crying. Tetanus is diagnosed on the basis of clinical features and does not require laboratory confirmation. Tetanus requires treatment in a medical facility, often in a referral hospital. People who recover from tetanus do not have natural immunity and can be infected again and therefore need to be immunized. Prevention: Tetanus can be prevented through immunization with tetanus-toxoid-containing vaccines (TTCV), which are included in routine immunization programmes globally and administered during antenatal care contacts. To be protected throughout life, WHO recommends that an individual receives 6 doses (3 primary plus 3 booster doses) of TTCV. The 3-dose primary series should begin as early as 6 weeks of age, with subsequent doses given with a minimum interval of 4 weeks between doses. The 3 booster doses should preferably be given during the second year of life (12–23 months), at 4–7 years of age, and at 9–15 years of age. Ideally, there should be at least 4 years between booster doses. There are many kinds of vaccines used to protect against tetanus: diphtheria and tetanus (DT) vaccinesdiphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis (whooping cough) (DTaP) vaccinestetanus and diphtheria (Td) vaccinestetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis (Tdap) vaccines.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Tetanus during childbirth, symptoms include stiffness of the body, which often leads to difficulty in breastfeeding the baby and excessive crying. Tetanus is confirmed based on symptoms without the need for testing. Tetanus requires hospital care, especially referral to a specialized hospital. People who have recovered from tetanus do not have natural immunity and may be re-infected, therefore they need booster shots. Prevention: Protection against tetanus can be obtained through vaccination with the toxoid, which is used during the planned rounds of global vaccination and given during antenatal care visits. If a person does not want to contract the disease permanently, the World Health Organization recommends that a person receive 6 doses of the tetanus toxoid vaccine (primary 3 and booster). The first 3 doses should be given consecutively, at 6 weeks and at birth, followed by a dose after 4 weeks. The 3rd booster is preferably given in the second year after birth (between 12 and 23 months), then at 4 to 7 years, and again at 9 to 15 years. It is preferable to have at least 4 years between booster shots. There are different types of vaccines that can be used to obtain protection against tetanus: tetanus toxoid vaccine during childhood, tetanus-diphtheria vaccines, and vaccines against pertussis.**

**Notes on the translation:**

*   I've aimed for clarity and accuracy while maintaining a natural English flow.
*   "Alluaran da ke kashe ta" is translated as "vaccination with the toxoid" as it refers to the tetanus toxoid vaccine.
*   "Riga-kafi" is consistently translated as "booster shot" or "booster."
*   I've tried to preserve the original structure and meaning of the Hausa text.

translations.source: Za a iya kauce wa kamuwa da cutar tetanos ta lokacin haihuwa, ta hanyar yi wa mata da suka kai shekarun haihuwa riga-kafi da alluran kashe cutar tetanos kodai a lokacin da suke da juna biyu ko bayan haihuwa. Haka kuma, yawan kula da lafiya a-kai-a-kai ka iya hana kamuwa da cutar tetanos, wanda ya haɗa tsafta lokacin haihuwa da kula da lafiyar jariri yayin haihuwa da kula da rauni wajen yin tiyata da kuma aikin haƙori.
translations.reference: Neonatal tetanus can be prevented by immunizing women of reproductive age with TTCV, either during pregnancy or outside of pregnancy. Additionally, robust medical practices can also prevent tetanus disease including clean delivery and cord care during childbirth, and proper wound care for surgical and dental procedures.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

"Tetanus infection can be prevented during childbirth by vaccinating women of childbearing age with tetanus toxoid vaccines, either when they are pregnant or after childbirth. Furthermore, regular health care can also prevent tetanus, which includes cleanliness during childbirth, caring for the newborn during childbirth, and proper care of wounds during surgery and dental work."

translations.source: Cutar kumburin ƙwaƙwalwa ƙarin bayani: Cutar ƙwaƙwalwa na ɗaya daga cikin muhimman abubuwan da suke haifar da cuta a ƙwaƙwalwa a gabashi da tsakiya da arewaci kuma yake ci gaba ƙaruwa yammacin ƙasashen Turai, sannan kuma a arwacin China da Mongolia da Ttarayyar Rasha. Nau'in ƙwayar wannan cuta na cikin dangin ƙwayoyin cuta na Filabibiraidi. Kimanin mutane 10,000 zuwa 12,000 ke kamuwa da cutar ƙwaƙwalwa a duk shekara, amma an yi imanin wannan adadi bai kai ainihin adadin da ake samu ba. Mafi yawan wannan cuta da ƙwayar cutar na samuwa ne sakamakon ticks da ya kamu da cuta, wanda yawanci yake kasancewa a jikin fata tsawon kwanaki. A wasu lokutan, ana kamuwa da cutar ta hanyar shan nonon akuya ko tunkiya ko saniya mai cuta da aka sha ba tare da an dafa ba. Mutane kan kamu da cutar kumburin ƙwaƙwalwa a yayin mu'amala a wuraren da suke da daji da nisansa ya kai kimanin mita 2000. Mutane ba su yaɗa cutar a junansu. Alamomi: Yawanci kamuwa da cutar ba ya nuna alama. cutar kumburin ƙwaƙwalwa na ɗaukar kwana 2 zuwa 28 kafin ta fara (Yawanci kwana 7 zuwa 14) sannan kuma da kwana 1 zuwa 8 wanda shi ne alama ta bai-ɗaya, inda alamomi irin su yawan gajiya da ciwon kai da rashin jin daɗin jiki, wanda ya wanci ya haɗa da zazzaɓi wanda zafinsa ya kai 38C.
translations.reference: Tick-borne encephalitis Overview: Tick-borne encephalitis (TBE) is an important cause of viral infections of the central nervous system in eastern, central, northern and increasingly western European countries, and in northern China, Mongolia, and the Russian Federation. Tick-borne encephalitis virus is a member of the family Flaviviridae. Approximately 10 000–12 000 clinical cases of tick-borne encephalitis are reported each year, but this figure is believed to be significantly lower than the actual total number of clinical cases. The vast majority of infections with the virus result from infected ticks, which often remain firmly attached to the skin for days. On rare occasions, infection can result from consumption of unpasteurized milk from infect goats, sheep or cows. People come in contact with the ticks during outdoor activities in forested areas up to an altitude of about 2000 meters. There is no direct person-to-person transmission. Symptoms: Most infections remain asymptomatic. In case of clinical illness, the incubation period for tick-borne encephalitis lasts 2–28 days (most commonly 7–14 days) and is followed by 1–8 days of general cold symptoms, such as fatigue, headache and general malaise, usually combined with fever of ≥38 °C.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**More Information on Tick-Borne Encephalitis (TBE):** Tick-borne encephalitis is one of the significant causes of inflammation of the brain in Eastern, Central, and Northern Europe, and it is increasingly spreading westward across European countries. It is also found in parts of Russia, China, and Mongolia. The causative agent of this disease is a virus belonging to the Flaviviridae family. Approximately 10,000 to 12,000 people contract tick-borne encephalitis each year, but it is believed that this number does not reflect the actual incidence. Most cases of the disease and the virus are transmitted through ticks that have been infected, which typically remain attached to the skin for several days. In some instances, infection can occur through the consumption of raw goat's milk, cow's milk, or contaminated meat that has not been cooked. People contract tick-borne encephalitis during interactions in areas with dense vegetation and a distance of approximately 2000 meters. People do not transmit the disease to each other.

**Symptoms:** Infection with the disease often does not show any symptoms. Tick-borne encephalitis takes 2 to 28 days to incubate (usually 7 to 14 days), followed by a period of 1 to 8 days which is the acute phase, where symptoms such as fatigue, headache, and general malaise appear, which may include fever with a temperature of up to 38°C.



I hope this translation is helpful!

translations.source: Bayan rashin nuna alama na kwana 1 zuwa 20, kusan kaso 15% na marasa lafiyar sukan fuskanci mataki na biyu na cutar, wanda ake yawan yin zazzaɓi da zafinsa ya wuce 40C da kuma nuna alamun cutar, kamar rashin jin ɗin jiki (da ya haɗa da zazzaɓi da ciwon kai ƙagewar wuya) da sakwarkwacewar ƙwaƙwalwa (kamar yawan bacci da rikicewa da rikicewar hanyoyin hankali) da zafin laka. Zafin ƙwaƙwalwa ka iya haifar da shanyewar ɓarin jiki da wata cutar ko ma mutuwa. Kimanin kaso 1% na matsalar da shafi ƙwaƙwalwa na iya gushewa, inda aka samu rahoton yawan mutuwa a Tarayyar Rasha, wanda za a iya dangantawa da ƙwayoyin cuta iri-iri. Ttsananin cutar na ƙaruwa, amma an samu rahoton samun mace-mace a kowane rukunin shekara. Babu wani takamaiman maganin kumburin ƙwaƙwalwa. Kariya: Mutane za iya samun kariya daga cutar tufafin ƙwaƙwalwa ta hanyar saka tufafin da ya dace, wanda ya haɗa da wando da safa, a yayin zuwa ko zama a ƙasashe ko wuraren da ke fuskantar barazana. Ya kamata a riƙa duba jiki kullum sannan a cire kumburin nan da nan. Ya kamata a guji shan madarar dabbar da ta kamu ba tare da an dafa a waɗannan wwuraren. Riga-kafi ya fi komai saurin yin maganin cutar. A yanzu akwai allurai 4 da suke da inganci yin maganin cutar:
translations.reference: After an asymptomatic interval of 1–20 days up to 15% of patients experience a second phase of the disease characterized by fever frequently exceeding 40 °C and signs of central nervous system involvement, such as meningitis (e.g. fever, headache, and a stiff neck), encephalitis (e.g., drowsiness, confusion, and sensory disturbances), myelitis or radiculitis. Encephalitis developing during this second phase may result in paralysis, permanent sequelae or death. About 1% of cases with neurological pathologies may die; higher fatality rates have been reported from the Russian federation, which may be related to a different virus subtype. Severity of illness increases with age of the patient, but fatalities have been reported from all age groups. There is no specific treatment for tick-borne encephalitis. Prevention: People can protect themselves from ticks by wearing appropriate clothing, including long trousers and closed footwear, when hiking or camping in countries or areas at risk. The whole body should be inspected daily and attached ticks removed as soon as possible. The consumption of unpasteurized dairy products should also be avoided in those areas. Immunization offers the most effective protection. Currently, there are 4 widely used vaccines of assured quality:

Brain fever can lead to the leakage of bodily fluids and other illnesses or even death. Approximately 1% of cases involving brain complications can be fatal, with reports of high mortality rates in the Russian Federation, which may be attributed to various viral strains. The severity of the disease is increasing, but cases have been reported in every age group. There is no specific treatment for brain swelling. translations.source: Sun haɗa da allurar FSME-Immun da Encepu, waɗanda ake yi a Austria da Germany, sannan kuma dangane da ƙarfin ƙwayar cutar a Turai da TBE-Moscow da kuma EnceVir, waɗanda da ake yin su a Tarayyar Rasha, sannan dangane da ƙarfinta a can gabashi. Ana ganin allurai 4 sun fi inganci da aminci. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta bayar da shawarar yin alluran ga kowane rukuni na shekara, da ya haɗa da yara a wuraren da cutar ta zama annoba. Cutar kumburin ƙwaƙwalwa na haifar da cutar Borreliosis, wadda ƙwayar cuta ke haifarwa. Allurar TBE ba ta maganin wannan cuta, wacce za a iya maganinta da maganin da ke kashe ƙwayoyin cuta.
translations.reference: FSME-Immun and Encepur, manufactured in Austria and Germany respectively, and based on European strains of the virus; and TBE-Moscow and EnceVir, manufactured in the Russian Federation and based on Far-Eastern strains. The 4 vaccines are considered to be safe and effective. In areas where the disease is highly endemic, WHO recommends that vaccination be offered to all age groups, including children. Ticks also transmit Borreliosis (Lyme disease), which is a bacterial infection. TBE vaccination is not effective against this disease, which however is treatable with antimicrobials.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

"They include FSME-Immun and Encepu vaccines, which are produced in Austria and Germany, and regarding the strength of the pathogen in Europe, and TBE-Moscow and EnceVir, which are produced in the Russian Federation, and regarding its strength there in the east. These 4 vaccines are considered to be the most effective and safe. The World Health Organization recommends vaccinating every age group annually, including children in areas where the disease is endemic. Tick-borne encephalitis causes Borreliosis, a disease caused by a bacterium. The TBE vaccine does not treat this disease, which can be treated with antibiotics that kill the bacteria."

translations.source: Taba sigari Ƙarin bayani: Sinadarin nikotin da ke cikin taba sigari na kama mutane, sannan shan sigari na ɗaya daga cikin manyan matsalolin da ke haifar da shanyewar ɓarin jiki da matsalolin numfashi, inda take haifar fiye da kaso 20% na ire-iren cutar kansa da sauran manyan cututtuka. A kowace shekara, mutane miliyan 8 ne suke mutuwa sakamakon shan taba sigari. Yawan mace-macen da ke da nasaba da taba sigari na faruwa ne a ƙaranci da matsakaicin tattalin arziki, waɗanda su kamfanonin taba sigari suka fin yin tallace-tallace. Haka kuma taba sigari na kashe waɗanda ba sa sha. Masu shaƙar hayaƙin taba sigari na fuskantar barazanar lafiya, inda kimanin mutane miliyan 1,2 ke mutuwa duk shekara. Kusan rabin yara ƙanana na sheƙar iskar da taba sigari ta gurɓata, sannan kuma kimanin yara dubu 65,000 ke mutuwa duk shekara sakamakon cututtuka masu nasaba da shaƙar taba sigari. Shan taba yayin da ake ɗauke da ciki na iya haifar da matsalolin lafiya na har abada ga jarirai. Kayayyakin taba da aka gasa (HTPs) sun ƙunshi taba, kuma suna jefa masu shau haɗuwa da hayaƙi mai guba, waɗanda da yawa daga cikin su ke haifar da cutar kansa kuma suna da illa ga lafiya. Tsarin isar da nicotine na lantarki (ENDS) da tsarin ba da abubuwan da ba na nicotine ba na lantarki (ENNDS), wanda akafi sani da e-cigarettes, basa ƙunshe da taba kuma ƙila su ƙunshi nicotine ko ba su ƙunshi nicotine ba, amma suna da illa ga lafiya kuma babu shakka amfani da su yana da haɗari.
translations.reference: Tobacco Overview: Nicotine contained in tobacco is highly addictive and tobacco use is a major risk factor for cardiovascular and respiratory diseases, over 20 different types or subtypes of cancer, and many other debilitating health conditions. Every year, more than 8 million people die from tobacco use. Most tobacco-related deaths occur in low- and middle-income countries, which are often targets of intensive tobacco industry interference and marketing. Tobacco can also be deadly for non-smokers. Second-hand smoke exposure has also been implicated in adverse health outcomes, causing 1.2 million deaths annually. Nearly half of all children breathe air polluted by tobacco smoke and 65 000 children die each year due to illnesses related to second-hand smoke. Smoking while pregnant can lead to several life-long health conditions for babies. Heated tobacco products (HTPs) contain tobacco and expose users to toxic emissions, many of which cause cancer and are harmful to health. Electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS) and electronic non-nicotine delivery systems (ENNDS), commonly known as e-cigarettes, do not contain tobacco and may or may not contain nicotine, but are harmful to health and undoubtedly unsafe.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Smoking: Further Information**

The nicotine substance in cigarettes is addictive, and smoking is one of the major causes of respiratory diseases and breathing problems, contributing to over 20% of cancer cases and other serious illnesses. Every year, 8 million people die as a result of smoking. The prevalence of smoking among women occurs more frequently in low-income and middle-income countries, where tobacco companies aggressively market their products. Smoking also kills those who don't smoke. People exposed to secondhand smoke face health risks, with approximately 1.2 million deaths occurring annually. Nearly half of young children are exposed to smoke from smoking, and around 65,000 children die each year from diseases related to secondhand smoke. Smoking during pregnancy can cause lifelong health problems for babies. Heated tobacco products (HTPs) contain tobacco and expose users to harmful smoke, many of which cause cancer and are harmful to health. Electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS) and electronic non-nicotine delivery systems (ENNDS), commonly known as e-cigarettes, do not contain tobacco and may or may not contain nicotine, but they are harmful to health and there is no doubt that their use is dangerous.

translations.source: Sai dai, ya yi wuri a ba da cikakkiyar amsa kan tasirin HTPs da/ko amfani da sigari na dogon lokaci. Tasiri: Kimanin mutane biliyan 1.3 a faɗin duniya suna amfani da kayan sigari, kashi 80% daga cikinsu suna cikin ƙasashe masu ƙarami ko matsakaicin karfi. Yin amfani da taba yana taimakawa wajen ƙaruwar talauci ta hanyar karkatar da kuɗaɗen amfanin gida daga buƙatun dole, kamar abinci da matsuguni, zuwa taba. Wannan halin kashe kuɗi yana da wahala a hana shi saboda taba tana da shiga rai sosai. Haka nan yana haifar da mutuwa da wuri da naƙasa ga manya masu tasowa a gidaje, wanda hakan ke haifar da raguwar kuɗin shiga na gida da ƙarin kashe kuɗi kan kiwon lafiya. Baya ga illar da taba ke yi kan lafiya, an ƙiyasta cewa jimillar kudin da ake kashewa kan shan sigari (daga kuɗaɗen da ake kashewa kan kiwon lafiya da asarar yawan aiki) ya kai dalar Amurka tiriliyan 1.4 a kowace shekara, kwatankwacin kashi 1.8% na ma’aunin GDP na shekara-shekara na duniya. Kusan kashi 40% na wannan kuɗi ya faru ne a ƙasashe masu tasowa, wanda ke nuna babban nauyin da waɗannan ƙasashe ke fama da shi. Ana amfani da wani ɓangare na harajin taba don yaƙar wannan tasirin, kuma ana kallon hakan a matsayin hanya mafi inganci wajen hana shan taba, musamman tsakanin matasa da masu ƙaramin ƙarfi. Ƙarin harajin da ya ƙara farashin taba da kashi 10 cikin 100 yana rage yawan shan taba da kusan kashi 4 cikin 100 a ƙasashe masu ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi da kuma kusan kashi 5 cikin 100 a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga. Martanin ƙuniyar WHO: Girman wannan bala'i ga ɗan adam da na tattalin arziƙi yana da ban tsoro, amma kuma ana iya hana shi.
translations.reference: However, it is too early to provide a clear answer on the long-term impact of HTPs and/or e-cigarette use. Impact: An estimated 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, 80% of whom are in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by diverting household spending from basic needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This spending behaviour is difficult to curb because tobacco is so addictive. It also causes premature death and disability of productive age adults in households thus leading to reduced household income and increased healthcare costs. In addition to the detrimental impact of tobacco on health, the total economic cost of smoking (from health expenditures and productivity losses together) are estimated to be around US$ 1.4 trillion per year, equivalent in magnitude to 1.8% of the world's annual gross domestic product (GDP). Almost 40% of this cost occurred in developing countries, highlighting the substantial burden these countries suffer. Tobacco taxes are applied in part to combat this effect and are seen as the most cost-effective way of curbing tobacco use, particularly among youth and low-income populations. A tax increase that increases tobacco prices by 10% decreases tobacco consumption by about 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO response: The scale of this human and economic tragedy is shocking, but it’s also preventable.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

However, it is premature to provide a comprehensive answer regarding the impact of HTPs (Heated Tobacco Products) and/or the use of cigarettes over a long period. Impact: Approximately 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, with 80% of them residing in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by diverting household income from essential needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This spending pattern is difficult to curb because tobacco is highly addictive. It also causes premature death and disability among working-age adults in households, which leads to reduced household income and increased spending on healthcare. In addition to the health consequences of tobacco, the total economic cost of smoking (including healthcare expenditures and lost productivity) is estimated at US$1.4 trillion annually, equivalent to 1.8% of the world’s annual GDP. Approximately 40% of this cost occurs in developing countries, highlighting the significant burden these countries face. A portion of tobacco taxes is used to combat this impact, and this is considered an effective way to prevent tobacco use, especially among young people and those with limited income. Additional taxes that increase the price of tobacco by 10% reduce tobacco consumption by approximately 4% in high-income countries and by roughly 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO's Response: The magnitude of this scourge on human and economic well-being is alarming, but it is preventable.

translations.source: Big Tobacco - tare da duk masu haɗa kayan sigari - suna yaƙi don tabbatar da cewa sun ɓoye haɗarin hajarsu, amma muma muna yaƙarsu: A shekarar 2003, Ƙasashe Mambobin WHO a jimlace sun amince da Yarjejeniyar Tsarin Taimako ta WHO kan Kula da Taba (WHO FCTC), yarjejeniya kan lafiyar jama'a ɗaya tilo a ƙarƙashin ƙungiyar WHO. Yarjejeniyar da ke aiki tun 2005, a halin yanzu tana da mambobi 181. Don taimaka wa ƙasashe wajen aiwatar da WHO FCTC, WHO ta gabatar da MPOWER, wani ƙunshin matakan da kayayyaki na ƙwarewa, wanda kowanne ya dace da a ƙalla tanadin WHO FCTC guda ɗaya. MPOWER yana haɓaka ƙarfin ƙasashe na aiwatar da matakai 6 don rage amfani da kayan sigari: sa ido kan tsare-tsaren amfani da taba da daƙile wa, kare mutane daga shan taba, ba da taimako kan daina shan taba, gargaɗi game da haɗarin taba, tilasta hana tallan taba, tallatawa da ɗaukar nauyi, da ƙara haraji akan taba.
translations.reference: Big Tobacco — along with all manufacturers of tobacco products — is fighting to ensure the dangers of their products are concealed, but we are fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States unanimously adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only public health treaty under the auspices of WHO. In force since 2005, it has currently 181 Parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one provision of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER builds the capacity of countries to implement 6 measures to reduce the demand for tobacco products: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco use, offer help to quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship and raise taxes on tobacco.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

Big Tobacco – along with all cigarette manufacturers – are fighting to ensure they hide the dangers of their product, but we are fighting them too: In 2003, WHO Member States collectively adopted the World Health Organization Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the first public health treaty under the WHO. The treaty, which came into force in 2005, currently has 181 parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of evidence-based measures, each corresponding to at least one WHO FCTC provision. MPOWER strengthens countries’ capacity to implement 6 measures to reduce tobacco use: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco smoke, help people quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship, and increase taxes on tobacco.

translations.source: Trachoma Bayani: Trachoma cuta ce ta ido kuma cutar da ke kan gaba wajen jawo makanta a duniya. Ɗaurarrun ƙananan ƙwayoyin cuta da ake kira Chlamydia trachomatis ne ke haifar da ita. Cutar na yaɗuwa ne daga mutum zuwa mutum ta hanyar haɗuwa da ruwan da ke fita daga ido ko hancin mai ɗauke da cutar kai-tsaye ko ba kai-tsaye ba; yaɗuwar da ba ta kai-tsaye ba ta haɗa da ɗaukar cutar a jikin wasu nau’ika na ƙuda. Yaran da ba su kai matakin shiga makaranta ba suna kamuwa da cutar sosaa. Samfuran tsare-tsare sun nuna cewa mutum na buƙatar cututtuka sama da 150 masu haɗari kafin ya haɗu da rikice-rikicen makanta na trachoma. Cutar ta Trachoma na yaɗuwa sosai a wasu daga cikin matalautan duniya, waɗanda ke zaune a yankunan karkara da lunguna, kuma ba su da isasshen ruwa da tsaftar muhalli da tsarin kiwon lafiya. Afirka ce nahiyar da cutar ta fi shafa, amma kuma tafi ko ina ƙoƙarin shawo kan matsalar. Haka nan ana samun cutar a Amurka ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Kudu da Asiya da Ostiraliya da Gabas ta Tsakiya. Ya zuwa2 ga Janairun 2020, ƙasashe 13 sun ba da rahoton daƙile cutar.
translations.reference: Trachoma Overview: Trachoma is a disease of the eye and the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by an obligate intracellular bacterium called Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person by direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges of infected people; indirect transfer includes carriage on the body of species of flies. Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection. Models suggest that an individual requires more than 150 lifetime infections to develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is endemic in some of the world’s poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas and have highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but it also has the most widespread control efforts. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of 2 January 2020, 13 countries had reported achieving elimination goals.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

**Trachoma Explanation:** Trachoma is an eye disease and is among the leading causes of preventable blindness in the world. It is caused by small organisms called *Chlamydia trachomatis*. The disease spreads from person to person through contact with fluid from the eyes or nose of an infected person, either directly or indirectly; indirect transmission involves picking up the infection on the bodies of flies. Children who have not reached school age are particularly susceptible to the disease. Epidemiological data shows that a person needs to experience more than 150 risk factors before they develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is highly prevalent in some of the world’s poorest communities, residing in rural and remote areas, and lacking adequate water, sanitation, and health systems. Africa is the continent most affected by the disease, but efforts are being made worldwide to combat the problem. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Oceania, and the Middle East. As of January 2020, 13 countries reported having eliminated the disease.

translations.source: A shekarar 2019 kaɗai, an yi wa mutane 92 622 aikin tiyata don babbar cutar ta trachoma, kuma an yi wa mutum miliyan 95.2 maganin da magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta. Duk da waɗannan nasarorin, cutar ta ci gaba da zama babbar damuwa ga lafiyar jama'a, tare da asarar kusan dalar Amurka biliyan 8 a kowace shekara wurin yin aiki saboda makanta da matsalar gani. Alamomi: Kamuwa da cutar na haɗe da canje-canje na kumburi a cikin conjunctiva da ake kira "trachoma mai aiki". Garkuwar jikin mutum na iya kawar da dunƙulen cutar guda ɗaya, amma a cikin al'ummomin da cutar tayi yawa, sake haɗuwa da ƙwayoyin cutar yana faruwa akai-akai. Bayan shekaru da yawa ana maimaita cutar ta trachoma mai aiki, cikin fatar ido zai iya zama mai tabo mai tsanani (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) har ta juya zuwa ciki tana saka gashin ido yana gogar ƙwayar ido (trachomatous trichiasis), wanda ke haifar da ciwo na kullum da rashin iya kallon haske sosai; wannan da kuma sauran canje-canjen ido na iya haifar da tabon cornea. Idan ba a yi maganin sa ba, yana iya haifar da haɗuwar dusashewar da ba za a iya gyarawa ba, tare da haifar da naƙasar gani ko makanta. Shekarun da wannan ke faruwa ya dogara da abubuwa da yawa, waɗanda suka haɗa da ƙarfin yaɗuwa na cikin gida. A cikin al'ummomin da masu cutar ke da yawa sosai, tana iya faruwa a lokacin yarinta, ko da yake farkon nakasar gani ya fi faruwa tsakanin shekaru 30 zuwa 40. Mata sun fi makanta sau huɗu fiye da maza, mai yiwuwa saboda yawan saduwa da yara masu ɗauke da cutar. Mutanen da ke zaune a cikin mafi talauci kuma mafi nisan al'ummomin Afirka da Asiya da Ostiraliya da Gabas ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Kudu da Tsibiran Pacific suna cikin haɗari.
translations.reference: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea. Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

In 2019 alone, 92,622 people received surgery for the disease trachoma, and 95.2 million people received antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease continues to be a major public health concern, with an estimated loss of nearly $8 billion US dollars each year due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with changes in inflammation of the conjunctiva called "active trachoma." The body's immune system can clear a single infection, but in communities where the disease is prevalent, reinfection occurs frequently. After years of repeated active trachoma, the inner lining of the eyelid can become severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) to the point where it turns inward, rubbing on the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), causing constant pain and light sensitivity; this and other eye changes can lead to corneal scarring. If left untreated, it can lead to irreversible blindness, resulting in vision loss or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors, including the intensity of domestic transmission. In communities where infected individuals are very numerous, it can occur in childhood, although the onset of vision loss is more common between the ages of 30 and 40. Women are four times more likely to experience blindness than men, possibly due to increased exposure to children with the disease. People living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Oceania, the Middle East, Central America, South America, and the Pacific Islands are at risk.

translations.source: Magancewa: Ana iya kare cutar ta trachoma ta hanyar rage haɗurran da ke da alaƙa da muhalli. Waɗannan sun haɗa da rashin isasshiyar tsafta, da cunkoso a gidaje, da rashin samun isasshen ruwa, da rashin isassu kayan tsaftar muhalli da damar amfani da su. Yawanci cutar na farawa a ƙananan shekaru, kuma tana ƙaranci kuma mafi gaharta idan ana ƙara yawan shekaru. An fi kamuwa da cutar ta hanyar iyali da hulɗa da masu fama da cutar. Maimaita cutar na haifar da mafi girman alamun cutar. Ana iya daƙile cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a ta hanyar amfani da dabarun SAFE, wanda ya ƙunshi: Tiyata don magance trachomatous trichiasis, matakin makanta na cutar; Magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta don kawar da cutar, musamman bayar da maganin nan mai suna azithromycin mai kashe ƙwayoyin cuta da yawa, wanda masana'anta ke bayarwa kyauta ga shirye-shiryen ƙasa ta hanyar International Trachoma Initiative; Tsaftar fuska; da kuma Inganta muhalli, musamman samun damar amfani da ruwa da tsafta mafiya kyawu.
translations.reference: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease. Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Control:** Trachoma can be controlled by reducing risk factors related to the environment. These include inadequate hygiene, overcrowding in homes, lack of access to sufficient water, and a lack of adequate environmental sanitation facilities and access to them. The disease usually begins in childhood and worsens with increasing age. Infection is most common through family members and contact with those suffering from the disease. Repeated infections cause the most severe signs of the disease. Trachoma can be controlled as a public health problem by using the SAFE strategy, which includes: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the scarring stage of the disease; Antibiotics to eliminate the disease, particularly the distribution of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is provided free of charge to national programs by pharmaceutical companies through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial hygiene; and Environmental improvement, especially access to clean water and sanitation.

translations.source: WHO ta yi amfani da dabarun SAFE a shekarar 1993, kuma tana ci gaba da aikinta na yin jagoranci da daidaita ayyukan ƙasa-da-ƙasa don kawar da cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a.
translations.reference: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.
translations.hypothesis: The WHO (World Health Organization) began using SAFE strategies in 1993, and continues its work of leading and coordinating national efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.

translations.source: Magungunan Gargajiya da na Ƙari da Haɗaɗɗun Magunguna Bayani: Maganin gargajiya Maganin gargajiya yana da dogon tarihi. Shi ne jimillar ilimi da ƙwarewa da ayyuka bisa ƙa'idoji da imani da gogewa na cikin gida ga mutane masu al’adu daban-daban, wanda za a iya bayaninsa da wanda ba za a iya ba, wanda ake amfani da su wajen kula da lafiya da kuma rigakafi da gano cuta da ingantawa ko magance rashin lafiyar jiki da ta ƙwaƙwalwa. Maganin Ƙari "Maganin ƙari " ko "maganin madadi " na nufin wani jerin tsarin ayyukan kula da lafiya waɗanda ba sa cikin al'adar ƙasar na kanta ko magungunan gargajiya kuma ba sa cikin gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya. Ana amfani da su a madadin magungunan gargajiya a wasu ƙasashe. Magungunan ganye Magungunan ganye sun haɗa da ganyayyaki da kayan ganyayyaki da gyara ganyayyayki da kuma kayan ganye da aka sarrafa, waɗanda ke ƙunshe da sassan tsirrai ko wasu kayan shuka ko duka. Tasiri: An ƙirƙira kuma an ƙaddamar da Dabarun Magungunan Gargajiya na WHO 2014-2023 don mayar da martani ga ƙudurin Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya kan magungunan gargajiya (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Traditional, Complementary, and Combined Medicines: An Explanation**

**Traditional Medicine:** Traditional medicine has a long history. It is a comprehensive system of knowledge and expertise, practices based on principles, beliefs, and experiences specific to different cultures, which can be explained or unexplained, and are used to care for health, prevent and detect disease, improve or address physical and mental health problems.

**Complementary Medicine:** "Complementary medicine" or "supportive medicine" refers to a range of healthcare practices that are not part of the country's own traditional practices or traditional medicines, and are not part of the mainstream healthcare system. They are used as an alternative to traditional medicines in some countries.

**Herbal Medicines:** Herbal medicines include leaves, herbal products, processed leaves, and herbal materials containing parts of plants or other plant substances, or both.

**Impact:** The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014-2023 was created and launched to respond to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).

translations.source: Dabarun na da nufin tallafawa ƙasashe mambobin ƙungiyar wuein samar da tsare-tsare na gaske da aiwatar da tsare-tsaren ayyuka waɗanda za su ƙarfafa rawar da magungunan gargajiya ke takawa wajen kiyaye lafiyar al'umma. Magance ƙalubalen, mayar da martani ga buƙatun da ƙasashe mambobi suka gano, da kuma haɓaka aikin da aka yi a ƙarƙashin dabarun maganin gargajiya na WHO: 2002-2005, dabarun da aka sabunta a lokacin 2014-2023 ya ba da ƙaimi fiye da wanda ya gabace shi don ba da fifiko ga ayyuka da tsare-tsaren kiwon lafiya, waɗanda suka haɗa magungunan gargajiya da na ƙari, da ayyuka masu aiki. Manufofin dabarun su ne: Don gina fage na ilimi don gudanar da aikin T&CM ta hanyar tsare-tsare na ƙasa waɗanda suka dace; Don ƙarfafa tabbatar da inganci da aminci da amfani da T & CM yadda ya dace kuma cikin tasiri ta hanyar kula da magunguna da ayyuka da masu aiki; Don haɓaka shiga ko ina a duniya ta hanyar haɗe ayyukan T&CM cikin gudanar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya da kula da lafiyar kai. Martanin WHO: an samar da rahoton na WHO na duniya game da maganin gargajiya da na ƙari na 2019 don cike giɓin bayanai abin dogaro, sahihi, kuma na hukuma daga Ƙasashe Mambobi na yankin T&CM. Wannan rahoto ya yi duba kan ci gaban duniya a T&CM cikin shekaru ashirin da suka gabata, kuma ya dogara ne kan gudummawar ƙasashe mambobin WHO guda 179. Ya ba da bayanai masu mahimmanci ga masu tsara manufofi, da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya, da jama'a don yin amfani da yiwuwar gudummawar T&CM ga lafiya da walwala.
translations.reference: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners; To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"The strategies aim to support member states in developing realistic plans and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicines play in safeguarding public health. Addressing challenges, responding to the needs identified by member states, and building on the work done under the WHO’s traditional medicine strategy: 2002-2005, the updated strategy for 2014-2023 provided greater focus on prioritizing health actions and plans, incorporating both traditional and conventional medicines, and operational activities. The strategy’s objectives are: To build a knowledge base for conducting T&CM (Traditional and Complementary Medicine) work through national plans that are appropriate; To strengthen the assurance of quality, safety, and efficacy of T&CM appropriately and effectively through the regulation of medicines, practices, and practitioners; To promote access everywhere by integrating T&CM activities into the delivery of health services and self-care. WHO’s Response: The 2019 WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine was produced to fill the data gap with reliable, accurate, and official data from Member States in the T&CM area. This report reviewed global progress in T&CM over the past twenty years and was based on the contributions of 179 WHO Member States. It provides essential information for policymakers, health professionals, and the public to leverage the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being."

translations.source: Dashe Bayani: Dasa tantanin halitta da tishu da gaɓoɓin mutane Dashentantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin jikin mutum na ceton rayuka da yawa kuma yana dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci inda babu tasiri madadin su. Cikin shekaru 50, dashe yayi nasara a faɗin duniya. Sai dai akwai babban bambanci tsakanin ƙasashe wajen samun damar dashen da ya dace da kuma matakin aminci da inganci da samun gudummawa da dashen tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu da gaɓoɓi. Abubuwan da suka dace na dashe su ne kan gaba. Musamman, rashin biyan buƙatun marasa lafiya da ƙarancin abin dashe na haifar da yiwuwar fataucin sassan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa. Dashen mutum Tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu don dasawa na iya ceton rayuka ko dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci. Misali corneal graft zai iya dawo da gani a makantar corneal;
translations.reference: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs. The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;
