Related document: A76/A/CONF./1 - Health of Indigenous Peoples First-ever resolution to accelerate action on drowning preventionThe delegates at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly agreed a resolution to accelerate action on global drowning prevention. The resolution requests Member States to assess their national drowning situation and to develop and implement multisectoral drowning prevention programmes. Drowning causes 236 000 deaths every year. It is a leading global cause of injury-related child deaths. Over the past decade, 2.5 million people died from drowning, and over 90% of those occurred in low- and middle-income countries. At the invitation of the United Nations (UN) General Assembly, WHO will coordinate actions within the UN system on drowning prevention and facilitate the observance of World Drowning Prevention Day on 25 July each year. WHO will also set up a Global Alliance for Drowning Prevention with organizations of the UN system, international development partners and nongovernmental organizations. To better understand the true burden and impact of drowning, the resolution further requests WHO to prepare a global status report on drowning prevention.

Related documents: A76/a/conf./1 - Indigenous Health - First resolution to accelerate the prevention of drowning. Representatives at the seventy-sixth World Health Assembly agreed on a resolution to accelerate the taking of preventive measures against global drowning. The resolution called on member states to assess their drowning situations and to develop and implement multiple drowning prevention programs. Drowning accidents cause the death of two hundred and thirty-six thousand (236,000) people each year. It is the number one cause of death worldwide among children associated with vulnerability. Over the past ten years, two and a half million people have died as a result of drowning, and over 90% of those who died died in low-income and middle-income countries. At the invitation of the United Nations General Assembly, the WHO will design activities within the United Nations system on how to implement drowning prevention and facilitate the celebration of World Drowning Prevention Day; on July 25th each year. The WHO will also establish a global partnership on drowning prevention that will include UN security groups, international development partners, and civil society organizations. To achieve a more comprehensive understanding of the true burden of the drowning issue, the resolution further requires the WHO to prepare a global status report on drowning prevention.**

translations.source: Samar da Sabon ƙudiri don haɓaka ƙoƙarce-ƙoƙarce a kan ƙaƙƙarfan ƙarancin abinci Wakilan sun amince bisa kudiri kan hanzarta ƙoƙarin hana karancin abinci mai gina jiki ta hanyar ingantaccen abinci mai inganci. Naƙasu da ake samu na rashin bitamin, da ma'adanai, musamman na folate, baƙin ƙarfe, bitamin A, da zinc, yana shafar kashi 50% na dukan yara masu shekaru kafin makaranta da kashi 67% na dukan matan da suka kai shekarun haihuwa a Duniya. ƙarancin rashin abincin da ke gina jiki na iya haifar da mummunan sakamako, gami da spina bifida da sauran lahani na bututun jijiyoyi. Babban sikelin abinci mai ƙarfi yana cikin mafita. Ta hanyar ƙara muhimman bitamin da ma'adanai dan inganta cikin abinci da kayan abinci masu mahimmanci, irin su alkama da garin masara, shinkafa, mai dafa abinci da gishiri daidai da tsarin amfani da ƙasa da ƙarancin abinci, ƙasashe na iya Kara gyare gyare tare da ƙara hana bayyanar ƙarancin ƙarancin abinci. Ƙudurin ya bukaci ƙasashe (membobin) su ƙara haɓaka manufofi kan samar da abinci tare da wasu ƙananan abubuwan gina jiki da/ko kari, da kuma yin la'akari da hanyoyin ƙarfafa shigowar kuɗi da hanyoyin sa ido. An amince da ƙudirin a karkashin yardar rahoton shekaru goma na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya kan abinci mai gina jiki (2016-2025). Alaƙantacciyar takardar da ke da lambar: EB152/CONF./5 Haɓaka ƙoƙarce-ƙoƙarce don kauda ƙarancin abinci mai gina jiki da sakamakonsu, da kuma spina bifida da sauran illolin bututun jijiya, ta hanyar ingantaccen abinci mai inganci. translations.reference: New resolution to accelerate efforts on food micronutrient fortification The delegates approved a resolution on accelerating efforts to prevent micronutrient deficiencies through safe and effective food fortification. Deficiencies in vitamin and mineral status, particularly of folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc, affect 50% of all preschool aged children and 67% of all women of reproductive age worldwide. Micronutrient deficiencies can have serious consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects.Large scale food fortification is part of the solution. By adding essential vitamins and minerals to staple foods and condiments, such as wheat and maize flours, rice, cooking oil and salt in accordance with national consumption patterns and deficiencies, countries can correct and further prevent a demonstrated micronutrient deficiency. The resolution urges Member States to develop policies on food fortification with micronutrients and/or supplementation, and to consider ways of strengthening financing and monitoring mechanisms. The resolution was agreed under the umbrella of the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025) report. Related document: EB152/CONF./5 Accelerating efforts for preventing micronutrient deficiencies and their consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects, through safe and effective food fortification Related links
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Providing Leadership for Enhanced Efforts to Address Severe Malnutrition**

Representatives have agreed on a resolution to accelerate efforts to prevent malnutrition through improved quality food. The prevalence of micronutrient deficiencies – vitamins and minerals, particularly folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc – affects 50% of all pre-school children and 67% of all women of reproductive age worldwide. Malnutrition can lead to severe consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects. Increasing the nutrient density of food is a key solution. By fortifying staple foods like wheat flour, maize, rice, cooking oil, and salt with essential vitamins and minerals, in line with national food systems and malnutrition prevalence, countries can make significant progress in preventing malnutrition.

The resolution calls on countries (member states) to further develop policies on food fortification with micronutrients and/or supplements, and to consider strategies for mobilizing funding and monitoring. The resolution was adopted under the auspices of the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025). Related document with the number: EB152/CONF./5

**Strengthening Efforts to Eliminate Malnutrition and its Consequences, including Spina Bifida and other Neural Tube Defects, through Improved Quality Food.**

translations.source: Ƙarin karatu da fahimta game da ƙudurin Ayyukan WHO domin inganta abinci Kasashe (mambobi) sun amince kan ayyukan faɗa da yaƙar magunguna marasa inganci A ranar 27 ga Mayu, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta saba'in da shida ta yarda kan wani ƙuduri na aiwatar da nazari mai zaman kansa na Mechanism (MSM) don abubuwan da suka faru, marasa inganci da magungunan jabu, wanda ke ƙarfafa kiran da Babban Darakta Janar na Janairu 2023 ya yi na a kara sa ido aiki a wannan yanki mai mahimmanci. Za a ƙaddamar da bitar a taron kwamitin gudanarwa na MSM na gaba daga baya a cikin 2023. Majalisar ta kafa MSM a shekara ta 2012 don kawar da magunguna marasa inganci da jabun samfuran likitanci ta mahangar lafiyar jama'a kuma ta hanyar gaskiya da haɗa kai. Manufar MSM ita ce ta kare lafiyar jama'a da haɓaka damar samun arha Mai aminci, inganci da samfuran ingatattun magungunan likitoci. Da yake lura da abubuwan da suka faru na baya-bayan nan, na gurɓatattun magunguna da aka samu a kasuwanni daban-daban, da kuma mace-macen da za a iya hana su, ƙasashe (mambobin) sun jaddada goyon bayan da ake bukata don inganta karfinsu na daƙile hakan, da kuma ganowa da kuma ba da amsa ga marasa inganci da gurbataccen kayayyakin kiwon lafiya; ta hanyar samun fasahohin da suka dace, gami da wuraren gwajin ɗakin gwaje-gwaje, tare da inganta sa-ido kan kasuwanni na yau da kullum da talla, da siyar da samfuran likitanci a kan layi. Kasashen (membobi) sun yi kira da a riƙa aiwatar da fasahohin bin diddigi don sarrafawa da yin kasuwa da sa ido, sannan akwai kuma buƙatar samar da haɗin gwiwa ta yau da kullun Kama daga abokan huldar gida da na duniya kamar hukumomin tilasta bin doka da kwastam. An ƙarfafa (membobin) ƙasashen su daidaita da ƙa'idojin da za su inganta doka da sanya takunkumin hana yin mu'amalan da ba su kamata ba a samfuran likitanci marasa inganci.
translations.reference: Read more about the resolution WHO's work on food fortification Member States approve actions to counter substandard and falsified medicines On 27 May, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly approved a resolution to conduct an independent review of the Member State Mechanism (MSM) for incidents, substandard and falsified medicines, reinforcing the Director-General’s January 2023 call for increased vigilance and action in this important area. The review will be initiated at the next MSM steering committee meeting later in 2023. The Assembly established the MSM in 2012 to tackle substandard and falsified medical products from a public health perspective and in a transparent and inclusive way. The goal of the MSM is to protect public health and promote access to affordable, safe, efficacious and quality medical products. Noting recent events of contaminated medicines found on various markets and related preventable deaths, Member States emphasized the support needed to improve their capacity to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products through access to appropriate technologies, including laboratory testing facilities, along with improved oversight on informal markets and advertising, and the online sale of medical products. Member States called for embracing and implementing track and trace technologies for market control and surveillance, as well as the need for formal collaboration by relevant local and global partners such as law enforcement and customs administrations. Member States were encouraged to align with conventions aimed at improvement of legislation and imposition of prohibitive sanctions for wrongful dealing in substandard and falsified medical products.

Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Further Reading and Understanding Regarding the WHO Action Plan to Improve Food**

(Member) countries have agreed on activities to combat substandard and falsified medicines. On May 27th, the Sixty-Sixth World Health Assembly approved a resolution to implement an independent review of the Mechanism (MSM) for addressing incidents, substandard and falsified medical products, reinforcing the call made by the Director-General in January 2023 to enhance surveillance efforts in this critical area. The initiative will be launched at the next MSM Executive Committee meeting later in 2023.

The Assembly established the MSM in 2012 to eliminate substandard medical products and counterfeit pharmaceuticals through public health approaches, transparency, and collaboration. The MSM’s objective is to protect public health and improve access to affordable, safe, quality, and efficacious medicines.

Noting recent incidents of contaminated medicines found in various markets and the potential harm they can cause, countries (members) emphasized the need for strengthened support to enhance their capacity to prevent, detect, and respond to substandard and falsified health products; by acquiring appropriate technologies, including reference standards and testing laboratories, as well as strengthening surveillance of markets and the promotion and sale of medical products online.

Countries (members) called for the implementation of traceability technologies for the control, marketing, and monitoring of products, and there is also a need to establish ongoing partnerships with domestic and international stakeholders such as law enforcement and customs agencies. Countries are urged to align their regulations to improve legislation and restrict transactions involving substandard medical products.

translations.source: Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta saba'in da shida - Sabuntawa kullum: 27 ga Mayu 2023 Dabarar farko ta duniya game da rigakafin kamuwa da cuta da kuma kula da Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince a yau kan dabarun farko na duniya kan rigakafin kamuwa da cuta (IPC), wanda ya gina kusan shekaru ashirin na kokarin da WHO da abokan tarayya suka jagoranta. Dabarar farko ta duniya game da rigakafin kamuwa da cuta da kuma kula da Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince a yau kan dabarun farko na duniya kan rigakafin kamuwa da cuta (IPC), wanda ya gina kusan shekaru ashirin na kokarin da WHO da abokan tarayya suka jagoranta. HAIS suna daga cikin abubuwan da suka fi faruwa a cikin yanayin isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya. Cutar sankarau ta COVID-19 da manyan cututtukan da suka barke a baya-bayan nan kamar cutar Ebola, Ciwon numfashi na Gabas ta Tsakiya da Cutar Cutar Sudan sun fallasa gibin da ke akwai a cikin shirye-shiryen IPC a duk ƙasashe. Dabarar ta tsara hangen nesa: Nan da 2030, duk wanda ke samun dama ko ba da kulawar lafiya ba shi da lafiya daga cututtukan da ke da alaƙa. Makasudai guda uku sune: don hana kamuwa da cuta a cikin kiwon lafiya; yi aiki don tabbatar da shirye-shiryen IPC suna aiki kuma an aiwatar da su; da daidaita ayyukan IPC tare da sauran fannoni da sassa. Dabarar ta mayar da hankali ne kan duk wani wuri da ake ba da kiwon lafiya, a cikin tsarin kiwon lafiya; Ya dogara ne akan ka'idar kulawa mai tsabta da aminci a matsayin muhimmin sashi na haƙƙin lafiya, wanda ke tafiyar da daidaito, wanda ya kamata ya tabbatar da lissafi da dorewa. Dabarun IPC na duniya za a cika su, kuma a yi amfani da su tare da haɗin gwiwa tare da, tsarin aiki na duniya mai alaƙa da tsarin sa ido, wanda za a haɓaka a cikin dubu biyu da ashi da uku 2023 - 2024. Takardun da ke da alaƙa EB152/9 - Daftarin dabarun duniya kan rigakafin kamuwa da cuta da sarrafawa, rahoton taƙaitaccen bayani na Babban Darakta-Janar.
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 27 May 2023 First global strategy on infection prevention and controlThe World Health Assembly agreed today on the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), which builds on almost two decades of efforts led by WHO and partners. The strategy provides Member States with strategic directions to substantially reduce the ongoing risk of health care-associated infections (HAIs), including those that exhibit antimicrobial resistance. HAIs are among the most frequent adverse events occurring in the context of health service delivery. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent major disease outbreaks such as Ebola virus disease, the Middle East respiratory syndrome and the Sudan virus disease have clearly exposed the existing gaps in IPC programmes in all countries. The strategy sets a clear vision: by 2030, everyone accessing or providing health care is safe from associated infections. Its three key objectives are: to prevent infection in health care; act to ensure IPC programmes are in place and implemented; and coordinate IPC activities with other areas and sectors. The strategy is focused on any setting where health care is delivered, across the health system; it is based on the principle of clean and safe care as a fundamental component of the right to health, which is equity driven, and which should ensure accountability and sustainability. The global IPC strategy will be complemented by, and used in conjunction with, an associated global action plan and monitoring framework, that will be developed in 2023–2024. Related document EB152/9 - Draft global strategy on infection prevention and control, Executive summary Report by the Director-General
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**76th World Health Assembly - Daily Update: 27 May 2023**

**The World’s First Global IPC Strategy**

The World Health Assembly today approved the world’s first strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), built on nearly two decades of effort led by WHO and partners. The World Health Assembly today approved the world’s first strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), built on nearly two decades of effort led by WHO and partners. HAIs (Healthcare-Associated Infections) are among the most common occurrences within the delivery of health services. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent major outbreaks like Ebola, Middle East Respiratory Syndrome, and Sudan Hemorrhagic Fever have exposed the gaps in IPC programmes in all countries.

The strategy outlines a vision: By 2030, everyone accessing or providing health care is safe from related infections. Three goals are: to prevent infection within health care; to work to ensure IPC programmes are functional and implemented; and to align IPC activities with other sectors and areas.

The strategy focuses on all settings providing health care, within the health system; It relies on the principle of clean and safe care as an essential part of the right to health, driving equity, which should ensure accountability and sustainability. Global IPC measures will be implemented and used in conjunction with a global framework for action linked to a monitoring system, which will be developed in 2023-2024.

**Related Documents**

EB152/9 - Draft of the global strategy on infection prevention and control and control, report by the Director-General.

translations.reference: WHO's work on infection prevention and control Historic resolution paves the way for strengthening rehabilitation in health systemsToday the World Health Assembly agreed a landmark resolution on strengthening rehabilitation in health systems. Rehabilitation services play a key role in ensuring the enjoyment of human rights including the highest attainable standard of physical and mental health. It also promotes sexual and reproductive health, and recognizes the right to work and the right to education. This landmark resolution aims to address the challenges in rehabilitation such as the need to: increase awareness of rehabilitation when setting health priorities and research agendas, allocating resources, promoting cooperation and enabling technology transfer; ensure countries are better equipped to respond to the sudden increase in rehabilitation needs including assistive technology due to health emergencies; ensure persons in marginalized and vulnerable situations have access to affordable, quality and appropriate rehabilitation services including assistive technology; avoid high out-of-pocket costs for people to access rehabilitation services and assistive technology that can cause financial hardships; and address the current insufficient level of rehabilitation workforce to serve the needs of the population. The resolution lists a range of actions to be taken by the WHO Secretariat such as:

translations.source: A ranar 26 ga Mayu Membobin ƙasashe sun amince da wani ƙudiri don ƙarfafa ƙarfin bincike a cikin ƙasashe da kuma inganta damar yin amfani da sakamakon binciken. Babban binciken da aka gudanar ya bayyana cewa binciken lafiya na da mahimmanci wajen kariya, sa ido, bibiya da kuma ɗaukar matakan magance cututtuka masu yaduwa, marasa yaduwa, ɗai-ɗaikun cututtuka, raunuka, da kuma naƙasa. Bincike na bada damar fahimta da kuma gano ainihin cututtuka, saboda haka, farawa da wuri na da muhimmanci domin samar da al'umma me cika da ingantattar lafiya. Ƙudurin yayi la'akari da cikakken nau'in bincike don haka ya haɗa da duka gwaje-gwajen ɗakin gwaje-gwaje, misali, gwaje-gwaje masu saurin ganowa da wasu nau'o'in na daban wanda ake ganowa misali ta hanyar hoto ko na'urorin auna hawan jini. Ya ƙunshi ayyuka don bincike da haɓaka masana'antu (ciki har da samarwa na gida da kuma hada-hada a zamanance), ƙa'ida, zaɓi da sayayya, wayar da kan jama'a, bayar da shawarwari da magance matsalolin samun dama gabaɗaya. aiwatar da ayyukan ze ƙunshi faɗaɗa ayyukan baya da wanda akeyi yanzu haka a matakai uku na WHO domin taimakon ƙasashe wajen inganta ayyukan duba majinyata. an buƙaci sakatariyar ta samar da rahoton aiwatarwa a shekarar 2025. takardu masu alaƙa EB152/6 inganta hanyoyi duba majinyata
translations.reference: On 26 May Member States endorsed a resolution to strengthen diagnostics capacity in countres and to improve access to diagnostic services. The broad ranging resolution recognizes that diagnostic services are vital for the prevention, surveillance, diagnosis, case management, monitoring and treatment of communicable, noncommunicable, neglected tropical and rare diseases, injuries, and disabilities. Diagnostics allow for the precise identification of diseases, and therefore the timely initiation of the correct treatments for better health outcomes. The resolution considers the full spectrum of “diagnostics”, thus including both “in vitro” laboratory tests e.g. rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and “non in vitro” diagnostics e.g. imaging or blood pressure measurement devices. It covers actions for research and development, manufacturing (including local production and technology transfer), regulation, selection and procurement, awareness, advocacy and addressing access barriers in general. The implementation of the resolution will build and expand on previous and current work at three levels of WHO to help countries improve access to diagnostic services. The Secretariat is requested to report on implementation progress in 2025. Related document EB152/6 Strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

"On May 26th, member states agreed on a resolution to strengthen research capacity within countries and improve the utilization of research findings. The main research conducted revealed that health research is essential for prevention, monitoring, tracking, and taking measures to address communicable, non-communicable diseases, injuries, and disabilities. Research enables understanding and identifying the root causes of diseases, therefore, early action is crucial for creating a community with improved health. The resolution considered a comprehensive type of research, including both laboratory tests, such as rapid diagnostic tests and other types that are detected, for example, through imaging or blood pressure measuring devices. It includes activities for research and development of industry (including local production and collaborative partnerships), regulation, procurement and selection, public awareness, providing advice and addressing overall access challenges. Implementation of the activities will involve expanding existing activities at the three levels of WHO to assist countries in improving health surveillance activities. The secretariat was requested to provide an implementation report in 2025. Related documents EB152/6 improving surveillance methods."

translations.source: Lambar yabo saboda gudunmawar da aka bayarwa kiwon lafiyar al'umma wanda aka gabatar a gurin taron lafiya na duniya karo na 76 A yayin babban taron lafiya na duniya karo na saba'in da shidda da aka yi a birnin Geneva, an bayar da kyautuka ga mutane da cibiyoyi daga sassa daban-daban na duniya domin yaba irin gudunmawar da suka bayar a fagen inganta lafiyar al'umma. Gangamin karrama wa da bada kyaututtuka na kula da lafiyar jama'a na 2023 gangami ne na yabawa kwazon al'umma da suka bada gudunmawa wajen inganta lafiyar al'umma a faɗin duniya, in ji Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta Janar na WHO. A dai-dai lokacin da duniya ke fuskantar ƙalubale kala-kala, kowannensu abin abun dubawa ne sannan kuma tunatarwar ce kan kwazok da za'a iya yi don inganta lafiya da jin daɗin kowa. Kowace shekara ana fitar da shelar naiman 'yan takara a matakin kowace kyauta bayan rufe taron Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya. Hukumomin lafiya na ƙasa wadda ke cikin rukunin lambobin WHO ko kuma wanda ya taɓa lashe gasar ne ke da alhakin gabatar da sunayen 'yan takara. A zamanta na 152 wanda ya gabata a watan Janairun 2023, Hukumar Zartaswa ta zayyana waɗanda suka lashe kyaututtukan na 2023, bisa shawarwarin da kwamitin da aka keɓe ya bayar na kowace kyauta. An zaɓo waɗanda aka karrama su bakwai ne domin nuna farin cikinsu da irin rawar da suke takawa gani da gudunmawar da suke bayarwa ga lafiyar al’umma a ƙasashensu da ma duniya baki ɗaya. Sun fito daga yankuna huɗu na WHO - Amurka, Gabashin-Mediterranean, Kudu maso Gabashin Asiya da Yammacin Pacific. Mutum biyar ne suka yi nasara, uku daga cikinsu mata ne.
translations.reference: Awards for outstanding contributions to public health presented during the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly During a moving ceremony at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly in Geneva, awards were presented to persons and institutions from around the world for their outstanding contributions to public health. “The 2023 public health prizes and awards celebrate people who have made a significant contribution to global health, and whose work has extended far beyond the call of normal duty,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “At a time when the world faces many challenges, each is an inspiration and a reminder of the progress that can be made to improve health and wellbeing for all.” The call for nominations of candidates for each prize is sent out each year after closure of the World Health Assembly. Nominations can be made by national health administrations of a WHO Member State and by any former recipient of the prizes. At its 152nd session in January 2023, the Executive Board designated the 2023 winners of the prizes, on the basis of proposals made by the dedicated selection panels for each prize. The seven laureates have been selected to celebrate their unique role and contribution for public health in their countries and globally. They come from four WHO Regions – for the Americas, the Eastern-Mediterranean, South-East Asia and the Western Pacific. Five winners are individuals, and three among them are women.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Recognition for Contributions to Public Health – Presented at the 76th World Health Assembly**

At the 76th World Health Assembly held in Geneva, awards were presented to individuals and organizations from various parts of the world to recognize their contributions to improving public health. The 2023 Public Health Champions recognition initiative is a celebration of the capabilities of communities who have made significant contributions to improving public health globally, according to Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO.

At a time when the world is facing various challenges, each of which is worthy of attention and serves as a reminder of the potential that can be harnessed to improve the health and well-being of everyone. A shortlist of nominees for each award is released at the level of each award after the World Health Assembly is concluded. National health authorities within the WHO framework or those who have previously won the award are responsible for submitting the names of nominees.

At its 152nd meeting in January 2023, the Executive Board announced the winners of the 2023 awards, based on the recommendations of a dedicated committee for each award. Seven individuals were selected to recognize their outstanding roles and contributions to public health in their countries and worldwide. They come from four WHO regions – the Americas, the Eastern Mediterranean, South-East Asia, and the Western Pacific. Five individuals were successful, three of whom are women.

translations.source: Bugu da ƙari, cibiyoyi biyun da suka sami lambar yabo ta 2023 mata ne ke jagorantarsu. Cibiyar Nick Simons daga Nepal, da Farfesa Vichai Tienthavorn daga Thailand, sune waɗanda suka lashe lambar yabo ta Sasakawa Health Prize a shekarar 2023 Cibiyar Nick Simons kungiya ce mai zaman kanta da ke rajin inganta harkokin kiwon lafiya a yankunan karkara na ƙasar Nepal. Tare da haɗin gwuiwar Ma'aikatar Lafiya da kula da yawan Jama'a na ƙasar Nepal, Cibiyar ta horar da ƙwararrun likitocin 7000 (kamar ungozoma da mataimakan masu aikin jinya) a yankunan karkara, waɗanda a yanzu ke aikin kula da majinyata a yankunan a duk lokacin da babu ƙwararru. Cibiyar ta samar da kayan aiki masu mahimmanci tare da samar da ƙarin ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya don rage rashin ƙwararrun likitoci a asibitoci 44, tare da tallafawa tsare-tsaren aiki don samar da ingantattun ayyuka a asibitoci fiye da 100 na yankunan karkara. Cibiyar ta kuma gudanar da ayyukan bincike da shawarwari domin tsare-tsare da sanin tasirin manufofin da suka shafi ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya na karkara a Nepal. Farfesa Vichai Tienthavorn shi ne Shugaban Cibiyar Praboromarajchanok a Ma'aikatar Kiwon Lafiyar Jama'a ta Thailand. Ɗaya daga cikin shirye-shiryen da ya jagoranta a faɗin ƙasar sun haɗa da samar da sabbin manufofi domin inganta kiwon lafiya don taimakawa wajen magance cutar thalassaemia, sakamakon aikinsa ƙasar ta ɗauki matakan kariya wajen gujewa da kuma yaƙar cutar thalassaemia. Ya kuma tsara ingantaccen kayan aikin sadarwa da hanyar tantancewa don taimaka wa 'yan ƙasa wajen kare kansu da kuma sarrafa ciwon sukari da hawan jini. A sakamakon wannan aikin, an samar da manufofi masu kyau da za su taimaka kwarai wajen magance cututtukan dake addabar ƙasar.
translations.reference: In addition, the two institutions that have received a 2023 award are led by women. Winners of the 2023 prizes Sasakawa Health PrizeThe 2023 laureates: The Nick Simons Institute from Nepal, and Professor Vichai Tienthavorn from Thailand The Nick Simons Institute is a nongovernmental organization aiming to improve health care in the rural areas of Nepal. Working closely with the Ministry of Health and Population of Nepal, the Institute has trained 7000 medical professionals (such as midwives and anaesthetic assistants) in rural areas, who now provide most-needed services to local communities in the absence of a sufficient pool of general practitioners and specialists. The Institute has supplied essential equipment and provided additional health personnel to alleviate the lack of medical professionals in 44 hospitals, and supported the design of an action plan for better quality services in more than 100 rural hospitals. The Institute also conducts research and advocacy activities to shape perceptions and influence policies that affect rural health workers in Nepal. Professor Vichai Tienthavorn is President of the Praboromarajchanok Institute at the Ministry of Public Health of Thailand. One of the nation-wide initiatives he has led is an innovative health promotion policy to help address thalassemia, with his work resulting in the adoption of an effective national policy for thalassemia prevention and control. He also designed an effective communications tool and screening method to help citizens to adapt their behaviours in order to prevent and control diabetes and hypertension. As a result of this work, a national policy was adopted with significant positive impact on the country’s disease burden.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"Notably, the two organizations that received the 2023 award are led by women. The Nick Simons Institute from Nepal and Professor Vichai Tienthavorn from Thailand are the recipients of the Sasakawa Health Prize for 2023. The Nick Simons Institute is an independent organization advocating for improved healthcare in remote areas of Nepal. In collaboration with the Ministry of Health and Population of Nepal, the Institute has trained 7,000 healthcare professionals (including nurses and assistant nurses) in remote areas, who are now providing care to patients in these areas whenever specialists are unavailable. The Institute provides essential equipment and additional healthcare personnel to mitigate the shortage of doctors in 44 hospitals, and supports operational plans to deliver quality services in over 100 remote area hospitals. The Institute also conducts research and advisory activities to plan and assess the impact of policies affecting healthcare workers in rural Nepal. Professor Vichai Tienthavorn is the Director of the Praboromarajchanok Institute at the Thailand Ministry of Public Health. One of the programs he led nationwide included developing new policies to improve healthcare to help address thalassemia, and as a result of his work, the country has taken protective measures to prevent and combat thalassemia. He also designed improved communication tools and screening methods to help citizens protect themselves and manage diabetes and high blood pressure. As a result of this work, good policies have been created that will truly help address the diseases that plague the country."

translations.source: Farfesa Tienthavorn ya kuma taka rawar gani wajen tinkarar ƙalubalen rashin daidaituwar rarraba ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya, musamman a kan iyaka da yankunan karkara na Thailand. Ya jagoranci haɗin gwuiwa tare da makarantun ƙauye domin ɗaukar ɗaliban manyan makarantu, horar da su da taimaka musu su zauna a gida bayan sun kammala ingantaccen tsarin karatun likitanci. Lambar yabo ta Gidauniyar Kiwon Lafiya ta United Arab Emirates 2023: Bayan kasancewarta sannana a fannin kiwon lafiya da bincike, zaƙaƙurar 'yar asalin ƙasar Philippines, Dr. Maria Asuncion Silvestre ta kasance me wayar da kan al'umma game da shayar da nonon uwa zalla sannan kuma ta samar da gidauniya mai zaman kanta Kalusungan Ng Mag-Ina (Lafiyar Uwa da Ɗa) a ƙasar Philippines. Dr. Silverstre tayi amfani da kwarewar ta a matsayin ma'aikaciyar lafiya wajen bincike da kuma fitar da tsare-tsare domin taimakawa wajen inganta lafiyar uwa da jaririnta. Tsare-tsaren data samar sun bayyana sauƙaƙan matakai da ya kamata jami'an kiwon lafiya dake kula da uwa yayin haihuwa da kuma jariri bayan haihuwa ya kamata subi. Wannan tsarin ya taimaka wajen samar da daidaito da inganta tsarin kula da yaro daga mahaifiyarsa. Tare da haɗin gwuiwar Gwamnatin ƙasar Philippines da kuma ƙasashe Sha Bakwai dake yammacin yankin pacific, WHO ta taka rawar gani wajen assasa gangamin wayar da kai dangane da muhimmanci kula da jarirai, shirine da WHO ta ƙirƙiro. Kyautar Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah kan binciken kiwon lafiyar tsofaffi da kuma wayar da kai cikin harkar Waɗanda suka lashe lambar yabo: Cibiyar Kula da Cututtuka da Cututtuka Marasa Yaɗuwa ta Ƙasa da Ƙasa daga kasar Sin; da Dr Abla Mehio Sibai daga Lebanon Cibiyar Hana Yaɗuwar Cututtuka da Cututtuka Marasa Yaɗuwa ta Ƙasa da Ƙasa, wadda wani ɓangare ne na Cibiyar Yaƙi da Cututtuka ta Ƙasar Sin (CDC), ta karɓi baƙuncin wani sashe da ke mai da hankali kan lafiyar tsofaffi tun daga shekarar 2013.
translations.reference: Professor Tienthavorn also played an essential role in addressing the challenge of uneven distribution of the health workforce, especially in border and rural areas of Thailand. He led the establishment of partnerships with village schools to recruit high-school students, train them and help them settle back locally after having accomplished an accelerated medical curriculum. United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Prize2023 award laureate: Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre from the PhilippinesIn addition to being a renowned clinician and researcher, Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre is a passionate advocate for exclusive breastfeeding and the founder of Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Health of Mother and Child) – a Philippine non-governmental organization. Dr Silvestre has leveraged her experience as a community practitioner to conduct research and design a protocol to help improve the health of mothers and newborns. The pioneering protocol she created describes a simple set of actions to be undertaken by health workers attending a mother during delivery and her newborn after birth. This protocol, which is applicable in all resource settings, helps to bridge the health equity gap in mother-and-child care. In partnership with the national government of the Philippines as well as 17 countries in the WHO Western Pacific Region, she has played a key role in scaling up the First Embrace campaign for Early Essential Newborn Care – a set of simple WHO-recommended and cost-effective interventions. His Highness Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Prize for Research in Health Care for the Elderly and in Health Promotion 2023 award laureates: The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention from China; and Dr Abla Mehio Sibai from Lebanon The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention, part of the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention (China CDC), has hosted a department focusing on the health of older people since 2013.

Professor Tienthavorn also played a significant role in addressing the challenge of unequal distribution of healthcare workers, especially in border areas and remote regions of Thailand. He led collaborations with rural schools to recruit high school students, train them, and help them stay in their communities after completing a comprehensive medical education program. The 2023 United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Award: Following her long and distinguished career in health and research, the Filipino native, Dr. Maria Asuncion Silvestre, has been a champion for promoting exclusive breastfeeding and established the independent foundation Kalusungan Ng Mag-Ina (Mother and Child Health) in the Philippines. Dr. Silvestre utilized her experience as a healthcare worker to research and develop plans to help improve the health of mothers and children. The plans she developed outlined simple steps that healthcare workers caring for mothers during childbirth and infants after birth should follow. This system helped to ensure equity and improve the system of childcare from the mother. In collaboration with the Government of the Philippines and seventeen Pacific Island nations, WHO played a key role in establishing an awareness campaign regarding the importance of infant care, a program created by WHO. The Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Prize for Health Research and Awareness: Among the awardees are: the National Center for Disease Control and Prevention of China; and Dr. Abla Mehio Sibai from Lebanon. The National Center for Disease Control and Prevention, which is part of the China Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), has hosted a section focused on geriatric health since 2013.

translations.source: Yana aiwatar da shirye-shirye da ayyuka da yawa waɗanda ke haɓaka kiwon lafiyar tsofaffi, kama daga ƙara fahimtar abubuwan da ke haifar da cututtuka da mace-mace ta hanyar sa-ido kan cututtukan da ba su yaɗuwa, bincike, rajista wa waɗanda suka mutu da binciken mutuwarsu, har zuwa shirye-shiryen da aka keɓance don haɓaka ilimin kiwon lafiya da manufofin da aka samar da su da hujjoji. Shirye-shiryen sun haɗa da kayan aikin da aka keɓe don tsofaffi, samar da ma'aikatan kula da lafiya na matakin farko guda 2000, makon ba da shawara kan kiwon lafiya da aka keɓe ga tsofaffi, da kuma wani sabon shiri don inganta lafiyar hankali da fahimi na tsofaffi fiye da rabin miliyan a cikin birane da yankunan karkara. Dr Abla Mehio Sibai farfesa ce a fannin ilimin cututtuka kuma shugabar tsangayar kimiyyar lafiya a Jami'ar Amurka ta Beirut da ke ƙasar Lebanon. Ta ba da gudummawa sosai ga lafiyar tsofaffi a Labanon da kuma yankin, tare da haɓaka ingantaccen tsarin yanayin tsufar mutane. Ta jagoranci wani sabon shiri na shigar da kusan tsofaffi 600 a kowace shekara a zuwa shirin karatu a Jami'ar manya a Jami'ar Amurka ta Beirut, baya ga horar da ƙwararrun kiwon lafiya. Tare da waɗansu, ta jagoranci babban wani gagarumin shiri na Ƙungiyar Lafiya ta mai suna Global Burden of Disease Study a Lebanon (2000) kuma ta gudanar da bincike na farko game da cututtuka marasa yaduwa da abubuwan masu kawo haɗari, da bayar da shawarwari, tsara manufofi da shirye-shiryen inganta kiwon lafiya. Don faɗaɗa nasarorin da ta samu, Dr Sibai ta haɓaka shirin inganta Dabarun Ƙasa don Tsofaffi a Lebanon (2020 “2030). Dokta Sibai ta kuma ƙirƙiro Cibiyar Tsofaffin Larabawa da Cibiyar Nazarin Tsufa don ƙirƙirar alaƙa tsakanin bincike da kuma samar da manufofi a matakan ƙasa, yanki da duniya baki-ɗaya. Kyautar Tunawa da Dr LEE Jong-wook Kyautar Tunawa ta fuskar hidima don Lafiyar Jama'a Mai karɓar kyautar a shekarar 2023: Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses daga Guatemala
translations.reference: It delivers a broad range of programmes and activities that advance health promotion for older people, ranging from better understanding the causes of morbidity and mortality through national surveillance for noncommunicable diseases, surveys, death registries and research, to tailored programmes to promote health literacy and evidence-based policies. Initiatives include a dedicated toolkit for older people, capacity‑building of 2000 primary care staff, a health advocacy week dedicated to older people, and an innovative project to improve the mental and cognitive health of more than half a million older persons in both urban and rural areas. Dr Abla Mehio Sibai is Professor of Epidemiology and Dean of the Faculty of Health Sciences at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon. She has made an outstanding contribution to older people’s health in Lebanon and the region, developing a holistic approach to the promotion of healthy ageing. She led the innovative initiative of enrolling close to 600 older people every year at the University for Seniors at the American University of Beirut, in addition to training public health professionals. She co-led the landmark WHO Global Burden of Disease Study in Lebanon (2000) and conducted the first national survey on noncommunicable diseases and risk factors, informing advocacy, policy-making and programme planning for health promotion. Building on her numerous achievements, Dr Sibai has developed the “National Strategy for Older Persons” in Lebanon (2020–2030). Dr Sibai also created the Arab Ageing Network and the Centre for Studies on Ageing to create links between research and policy at the national, regional and international levels. Dr LEE Jong-wook Memorial Prize for Public Health 2023 award laureate: Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses from Guatemala
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"He is implementing numerous programs and activities that promote the health of the elderly, ranging from increasing understanding of the causes of diseases and non-communicable diseases through screening, investigation, registration of the deceased and post-mortem examination, to programs specifically designed to improve health literacy and evidence-based policies. The programs include dedicated resources for the elderly, training 2000 primary healthcare workers, a weekly health counseling session dedicated to the elderly, and a new program to improve the mental health and well-being of over half a million elderly people in urban and rural areas. Dr. Abla Mehio Sibai is a professor in the field of epidemiology and head of the health science cluster at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon. She has made significant contributions to the health of the elderly in Lebanon and the region, with the development of an improved system for tracking mortality. She leads a new program to enroll approximately 600 elderly people each year into a study program at the university's graduate school at the American University of Beirut, in addition to training healthcare professionals. Together with others, she led a major Global Burden of Disease Study project for the World Health Organization in Lebanon (2000) and conducted the first research on non-communicable diseases and risk factors, providing advice, designing policies and health improvement programs. To expand on her achievements, Dr. Sibai developed the program to improve the National Strategy for the Elderly in Lebanon (2020-2030). Dr. Sibai also created the Arab Geriatric Center and the Center for Aging Studies to create a link between research and policy development at the national, regional and global levels. The Dr. LEE Jong-wook Memorial Prize for Public Health Service – Recipient for 2023: Dr. Jorge Francisco Meneses from Guatemala."

translations.source: Bayan da ya inganta haɗin gwiwar al'umma a wajen kulawar asibiti fiye da shekaru 20, Dokta Jorge Francisco Meneses ya kafa kwamitocin al'umma da ke mai da hankali kan aminci da gamsuwar marasa lafiya, iyalansu da kuma al'umma. Ganin irin tasirin da aikinsa ke da shi wajen inganta ingancin kulawar marasa lafiya, tsarin kula da aiki da kula da marasa lafiya wanda ya tsara ya samu damar zuwa har matakin ƙasa baki-ɗaya. A zamanin yau, a matsayinsa na mai kula da asibitocin Guatemala, ya tsara wani shiri don haɓaka ingancin aikin hidima ga majinyatan asibiti da iyalansu. Kwanan nan Dr Meneses ya shirya gwada wani sabon aikin a yankin kiwon lafiya ta Kudu ta Guatemala don ganin haɗin gwiwar cibiyar kiwon lafiya ta farko ta ƙasa, tare da mai da hankali kan al'ummomin da ba a nuna musu kulawa. Ayyukansa sun haifar da ɗaukar dabarun ƙasa na haɗin gwiwar cibiyoyi na kiwon lafiya, na farko a Amurka ta Tsakiya. Kyautar Nelson Mandela don Inganta Lafiya 2023 mai karɓar lambar yabo: Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma daga Bahrain Baya ga kasancewarta babbar jami'ar hukumar kula da harkokin kiwon lafiya ta kasar Bahrain, Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma ta riƙe muƙaman jagoranci daban-daban a cibiyoyin gwamnati da na ilimi a ƙasarta. Dokta Al Jalahma ta taka muhimmiyar rawa wajen samar da dabaru da tsare-tsare na ƙasa da ƙasa da dama a fannin kiwon lafiya a matakin farko, lafiyar yara da matasa, cututtuka masu tsanani da kuma hana shan taba. Daga cikin rawar da take takawa a halin yanzu, Dr Al Jalahma ta jagoranci manyan tsare-tsare masu nasara, kamar tsarin ba da izini ga cibiyoyin kiwon lafiya na kasa, canji zuwa fasahar zamani na Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta Kasa, da kuma tsare-tsare da yawa da ke haifar da ƙarin inganci a ɓangaren kiwon lafiya.
translations.reference: Having promoted community participation in hospital care for over 20 years, Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses set up community-based committees focusing on the safety and satisfaction of patients, their families and the community. Given the positive impact of his work in improving the quality of patient care, the model of ethical and patient-centred care that he designed was scaled up at the national level. Nowadays, as the national supervisor for hospitals of Guatemala, he has designed a programme to improve the quality of services for hospital patients and their families. Dr Meneses has recently coordinated an innovative pilot project in the Guatemala South Health Area to build the first national integrated network of health services, with a priority focus on underserved communities. His work led to the adoption of a national strategy of integrated health services networks, the first in Central America. Nelson Mandela Award for Health Promotion 2023 award laureate: Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma from Bahrain In addition to being the Chief Executive Officer of the National Health Regulatory Authority of Bahrain, Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma has held various leadership positions in government and academic institutions in her country. Dr Al Jalahma has played a major role in the development of numerous national and region-wide strategies and plans in the field of primary healthcare, child and adolescent health, chronic diseases and antismoking. In her current role, Dr Al Jalahma has led major successful initiatives, such as the national accreditation system for health institutions, the digital transformation of the National Health Regulatory Authority, and several initiatives leading to increased efficiency and excellence in the health sector.

Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

"After improving community collaboration in hospital care for over 20 years, Dr. Jorge Francisco Meneses established community committees focused on the safety and well-being of patients, their families, and the community. Recognizing the impact of his work in improving the quality of patient care and the patient care management system he designed, it was able to reach the national level. Currently, as supervisor of hospitals in Guatemala, he has designed a program to improve the quality of service for hospital patients and their families. Recently, Dr. Meneses prepared to test a new initiative in the Southern Health Region of Guatemala to foster collaboration between the first national health center, focusing on underserved communities. His efforts have led to the adoption of a national strategy for health center collaboration, the first in Central America. The Nelson Mandela Health Advancement Award 2023 Recipient: Dr. Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma from Bahrain In addition to being a senior official of the health regulatory authority of Bahrain, Dr. Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma holds various leadership positions in government and educational institutions in her country. Dr. Al Jalahma has played a key role in developing several national strategies and plans in the field of primary health care, child and adolescent health, serious diseases, and tobacco prevention. Among her current roles, Dr. Al Jalahma leads major successful programs, such as the licensing system for national health facilities, the transition to modern technology of the National Health Authority, and numerous plans that generate added value in the health sector."

translations.source: Har ila yau, tana da babbar murya wajen haɓaka kiwon lafiya a kafafe daban-daban a duk ƙasashen Majalisar Haɗin Gwiwar Gulf.
