translations.source: Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya sun kai wani jirgin sama mai dauke da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya zuwa Sudan Haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) sun kai wani jirgin sama mai ɗauke da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya zuwa ƙasar Sudan. wani jirgi ɗauke da kayayyakin jinya da tiyatar gaggawa da muhimman magunguna sun isa filin jirgin saman port Sudan da safiyar yau. kayan waɗanda darajar su ta kai dalar Amurka 444 000, shine na farko da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta iya kaiwa ta jirgin sama zuwa Sudan tun bayan ɓarkewar rikicin. Hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta rarraba kayayyakin kowon lafiya kafin ƙaruwar rikicin. Waɗancan sun ƙare ne ƴan kwanaki kaɗan bayan an bada adadin waɗanda suka jikkata. Aikewa da jirgin na zuwa ne a wani mataki na cigaba da bada agajin da haɗaɗaɗiyya daular larabawa(UAE) ke yi na bada tallafawa wa al’ummar Sudan da ke nuna ƙarfafa dangatakar ta da ƙasar Sudan, musamman ma ta la’akari da halin da ƙasar ke ciki a halin yanzu. Har ila yau faɗaɗa ayyukan jinƙai na haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa tare da jadadda alaƙar ta da sauran ƙasashe dangane da ƴan uwantaka da jajircewarta na taimakon wasu a lokutan rikici da halin gaggawa. Ƙaramar ministar haɗin gwiwar ƙasa da ƙasa Dakta Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy tace haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa (UAE) na cigaba da yin aiki kafaɗa da kafaɗa da hukumar lafiya ta duniya domin tabbatar da nasarar tura muhimman ayyuka da dabarun ayyukan agajin gaggawa da magunguna da abinci don taimakawa wajen magance halin da ake ciki a yanzu na rikicin Sudan. A daidai lokacin da ake kira ga dukkan rikice rikice da juna da su gaggauta tsagaita wuta tare da kawar da matsalar da ke janyo wa al'ummar Sudan wahalhalun da ba za a iya mantawa da su ba, Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa ta tsaya tsayin daka wajen bayar da taimako da taimako ga kasashe a lokutan bukata.
translations.reference: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict. These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**The United Arab Emirates and the World Health Organization have delivered an aircraft carrying essential medical supplies to Sudan.** The United Arab Emirates and the World Health Organization (WHO) have delivered an aircraft carrying essential medical supplies to the country of Sudan. An aircraft carrying emergency medical supplies and essential medicines arrived at Port Sudan airport this morning. The supplies, valued at US$444,000, are the first the World Health Organization (WHO) has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. The World Health Organization (WHO) had distributed health supplies before the escalation of the conflict. These were depleted just a few days after the number of injured was announced. The aircraft delivery comes as part of the ongoing humanitarian aid provided by the United Arab Emirates (UAE) to support the people of Sudan, demonstrating a strengthening of relations with the country, especially considering the current situation in the country. It also expands the humanitarian activities of the United Arab Emirates, strengthening its relations with other countries based on brotherhood and its commitment to assisting others in times of crisis and emergency. The Minister of State for International Cooperation, Dr. Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, said that the United Arab Emirates (UAE) continues to work hand-in-hand with the World Health Organization to ensure the success of delivering essential operations and strategies for emergency relief, medicines, and food to help address the current crisis situation in Sudan. At a time when all parties are being called upon to urgently cease fire and eliminate the problem that is causing the people of Sudan suffering that will not be forgotten, the United Arab Emirates stands firm in providing assistance and support to countries in times of need.

translations.source: Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa ta mayar da hankali ne musamman wajen samar wa kungiyoyin da suka fi fama da matsalar halin da ake ciki a Sudan, musamman ma marasa lafiya, da yara, da tsoffi, da mata wadanda suka fi fuskantar hadarin daga rikice-rikicen da ke ci gaba da faruwa. mafi matsananciyar gibi a cikin samar da magunguna da abinci. Ƙididdigar jin ƙai na Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa tana nufin cewa ta tabbatar da cewa ta ci gaba da isar da saƙo ga duniya ƙaƙƙarfan sadaukarwarta ga ƙarfafa zaman lafiya, tsaro da kwanciyar hankali, ba kawai a wani yanki ba, har ma a duk faɗin duniya, tare da abokan hulɗarta da al'ummomin duniya. kasar na ci gaba da taimakawa al'ummar Sudan a lokutan rikici. Kayayyakin kiwon lafiyar sun haɗa da isassun raunuka, kayan aikin tiyata na gaggawa, da magunguna masu mahimmanci don kai ga mutane 165,000 da ke cikin matsananciyar buƙatar agajin jin kai. Cibiyoyin kiwon lafiya a faɗin ƙasar sun ba da rahoton cewa, kayayyakin kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum sun ƙare kuma basa samun damar shiga shaguna saida magunguna a ƙasar. Daga cikin tallafin hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) ta tura ƙwararrun likitocin gaggawa guda biyu da ke tafiya tare da tabbatar da cewa an rarraba waɗannan kayayyaki nan take zuwa manyan cibiyoyin kiwon lafiya goma sha uku don tallafawa wa ma’aikatan kiwon lafiya da kuma bada kulawa ga masu buƙatar agaji. Hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) tana da tan talatin na kayan agaji na zazzaɓin cizon sauro da cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa kamar ciwon suga hawan jini da kuma yanayin da ka iya haifar da asarar rai matuƙar ba’a ɗauki mataki ba. An gama shirya waɗannan haɗe da wajen ledar jini 23,000 a kamfanin jigila na duniya na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya a International Humanitarian City, sannan Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya tana ta neman hanyoyin da za ta kai waɗannan kaya zuwa Sudan da gaggawa, tare da haɗin guiwar Ma'aikatar Lafiya ta Sudan. Babban daraktan shirin agajin gaggawa ta hukumar lafiya ta duniya Dakta Micheal Ryan yace shirin agajin gaggawa na kamafani na jigila na duniya na hukumar lafiya ta duniya da haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa ta zama cibiyar samar da taimakon gaggawa a faɗin duniya. Taimakawa wa ƙasashe sama da ɗari da arba’in na a yankuna shida na hukumar lafiya ta duniya kamfanin na samar da hanyar rayuwa ga mambobin jihohin da ke fuskantar matsalolin kiwon lafiya da suka taso sanadiyyar ɓarkewar cututtuka bala’o’i kamar ambaliyar ruwa girgizar ƙasa da kuma tasirin rikicin akan tsarin kiwon lafiya. Tallafin da haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa ke bayarwa ya baiwa hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) kasancewa a sahun gaba a matakin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya da kuma isar da ƙwararrun likitoci ga masu buƙata a ko’ina a kuma duk lokacin da ake buƙata.
translations.reference: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need. WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"The United Arab Emirates has particularly focused on providing assistance to groups most affected by the current situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most vulnerable to the ongoing conflict. The most pressing need is for the provision of medicines and food. The UAE's humanitarian aid agency aims to ensure it continues to convey to the world its firm commitment to promoting peace, security, and stability, not only in a specific region but across the globe, with its partners and communities worldwide. The country continues to support the people of Sudan during times of crisis. The medical supplies include sufficient bandages, emergency surgical equipment, and essential medicines to reach 165,000 people in dire need of humanitarian assistance. Health facilities across the country report that routine medical supplies have run out and are not available in pharmacies unless medicines are imported into the country. As part of the World Health Organization's (WHO) support, two emergency medical specialists have been deployed to ensure the prompt distribution of these supplies to thirteen major health facilities to support healthcare workers and provide care to those in need. The WHO has a stockpile of aid for yellow fever and non-communicable diseases such as diabetes and high blood pressure, and conditions that can lead to loss of life if no action is taken. These supplies, along with 23,000 blood bags, have been prepared at the World Health Organization's Global Logistics Hub in International Humanitarian City, and the WHO is seeking ways to deliver these supplies to Sudan urgently, in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. The WHO's Director of Emergency Preparedness and Response, Dr. Michael Ryan, said that the WHO's emergency logistics hub, in partnership with the United Arab Emirates, has become a global center for emergency relief. It provides a lifeline to populations in six WHO regions facing health challenges arising from disease outbreaks, disasters, floods, earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the United Arab Emirates has enabled the World Health Organization (WHO) to be at the forefront of emergency health response and to deploy specialists to those in need, anywhere and at any time."

translations.source: A ƙoƙarinta na gana ƙaƙƙarfan tsarin agajin gaggawa a faɗin duniya kulawa da kuma jajircewa haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa (UAE) da kuma hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) da abokan haɗin gwiwar suna aiki don isar da kayayyakin don isa ga mutane masu rauni a duniya a lokacin da suke kan buƙata A nasa ɓangaren Daktan hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) na yankin gabashin bahar rum Dakta Ahmed Al-Mandhari yace hukumar lafiya na cigaba da haɗa kai da hukumomin kiwon lafiya na Sudan domin isar da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya daga dukkan hanyoyin da ake da su. Sakamakon rikicin asibitoci kantinan magani da cibiyoyin kula da lafiya a matakin farko sun tanadi muhimman maguguna don ceton rai. Zuwan ƙarin tan talatin na kayyakin agaji da tiyatar gaggawa da kuma muhimman magunguna zasu ba hukumar lafiya ta duniya damar tallafawa hukumomin lafiya goma sha uku da sake kafa ayyukan kula da lafiyar masu buƙatar hakan. Kamfanin mu na jigila na duniya a Dubai yana taka muhimmiyar rawa wurin tabbatar kayayyakin kiwon lafiya ba tare da katsewa ba kuma muna godiya da tallafin da haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa ta bamu don sauƙaƙe mana na farko. Irin wannan taimakon kamar wannan shi ne yake nuna ƙoƙari a aikacenmu na yanki na samar da lafiya ba kowa. Tallafin lafiya da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta bayar ta hannun haɗaɗɗiyar fdaular larabawa ya kai kusan kashi hamsin da biyar na jimilla kayayyakin jinya da aka baiwa Sudan daga ƙetare a shekarar 2022 wanda ke nuni da matsayin haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa a matsayin babbar cibiyar taimakon jinƙai ta ƙasa da ƙasa.
translations.reference: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies. It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"In its efforts to establish a robust emergency response system globally, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization (WHO), along with their partners, are working to deliver essential supplies to vulnerable people around the world when they are in need.  The WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, Dr. Ahmed Al-Mandhari, stated that the World Health Organization is continuing to coordinate with Sudanese health authorities to deliver vital health supplies through all available channels.  The consequences of the conflict – hospitals, medicine stores, and health facilities – initially provided essential resources for saving lives. The arrival of an additional thirty tons of emergency relief supplies and essential medicines will enable the World Health Organization to support thirteen health authorities and restore healthcare services for those in need. Our global logistics company in Dubai is playing a crucial role in ensuring the uninterrupted delivery of health supplies, and we appreciate the support provided by the United Arab Emirates, which facilitates our efforts.  Support like this demonstrates commitment to our regional goal of providing healthcare for all. The health support provided by the United Arab Emirates through the World Health Organization (WHO) accounts for approximately fifty-five percent of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan since 2022, highlighting the United Arab Emirates' position as a major international humanitarian aid hub."

translations.source: Haɗin kai a cikin gida shine mabuɗin don ci gaba a duniya game da kiwon lafiya na muradan ƙarni SDGs Hukumomin 13 da suka sanya hannu kan Tsarin Ayyukan Duniya don Rayuwar Lafiya da Jin Dadin Kowa (SDG3 GAP) ne ke fitar da wani sabon rahoton ci gaba tare a haɗin gwiwa a yau. Rahoton mai taken me ya faru? mene bai yi ba? menen na gaba yana nuna shekaru huɗu na ayyukan haɗin gwiwa da manyan hukumomin ƙungiyoyi suka yi tare da bayyana abinda yayi aiki da wanda bai yi aiki ba domin ƙarfafa haɗin gwiwa da tallafi domin cigaban ƙasa a ɓangaren harkokin lafiya da kuma cigaban muradan ƙarni. A wannan shekarar ce aka kai rabin cimma cigaban muradan ƙarni amma kuma duniya tana baya wurin cimma muradan ƙarnin. Tun lokacin da aka ƙaddamar da shi a cikin 2019, SDG3 GAP ya ba da damar ƙirƙirar sabbin tsarin haɗin gwiwa tsakanin hukumomin da suka sanya hannu a cikin mahimman fannoni kamar dorewar kuɗi da kula da lafiya na farko da sauransu. Mafi ƙaranci ƙasashe 67 ne suka shiga ɗaya ko fiye da ɗaya daga cikin shirye-shiryen ingantata fasali na SDG3 GAP. Mun samu cigaba mai muhimmanci amma akwai amma har yanzu akwai doguwar tafiya domin inganta yadda ƙungiyoyi daban daban ke aiki tare domin tallafa wa ƙasashe. Dole ne mu saurari abinda ƙasashe ke gaya mana kuma muyi aiki bisa jagorancin su inji Dakta Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus darakta janar na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO kuma shugaban ƙungiyar shugabannin SDG3 GAP. Ina godiya ga abokan hulɗa bisa hadin kaida suka yi da kuma yadda suka nuna gaskiya cikin wannan rahoto. Rahoton ya bada shawarwari guda shida masu muhimmanci don tabbaatar da cewa ƙasashe sun ci gajiyar tallafin da ya dace da kuma tabbatar da alƙawuran da aka yi a SDG3 GAP.
translations.reference: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others. At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**Collaboration within countries is the key to global progress on achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) related to health. The 13 signatory authorities of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being (SDG3 GAP) are releasing a new progress report together today.**

**The report, titled "What happened? What didn't happen? What's next," highlights four years of collaborative efforts by leading organizations, outlining what has worked and what hasn't in strengthening collaboration and support for national progress in health and achieving the SDGs. This year marks the halfway point in achieving the SDGs, but the world is still behind schedule in meeting them.**

**Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP has enabled the creation of new collaborative partnerships among signatory authorities in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care, among others. At least 67 countries have joined one or more of the SDG3 GAP’s enhancement programs. We have made significant progress, but there is still a long way to go to improve how different organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries are telling us and work under their leadership, said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO) and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Leaders Group.**

**“I thank our partners for their collaboration and for their honesty in this report.” The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from appropriate support and fulfill the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.**



I hope this translation is helpful!

translations.source: Sun haɗa da: Cigaba da inganta cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG3 GAP na lafiya ta hanyar neman ra’ayoyin mambobin ƙasashe kan yadda muke haɗa kai a matakin ƙasa da kuma amsa shawarwarin da suka danganci; Kula da GAP a matsayin dandamali da haɗin gwiwa; Haɓaka haɗin gwiwa mai ƙarfi a matakin ƙasa kan kula da hukumar kiwon lafiya a matakin farko da kuma bincika sabbin batutuwa kamar tsarin kiwon lafiya mai jure yanayi Tare da yin amfani da sabbin hanyoyin a matakin ƙasa domin isar da tsari mai tasiri; Asusun Yara na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNICEF); Unitaid; Aiki tare da mambobin ƙasashe don ƙarfafa gwiwa ta hanyar jagoranci a siyasance da kuma jagoranci na shugabanci da kudi domin tallafawa da haɗin gwiwa. Mihimman binciken da aka samu na rahoton da aka samu na rahoton zai bada labari game da tattaunawar babban taron majalisar dinkin duniya na cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG da kuma taron duniya na akan kiwon lafiya a watan Satumbar 2023. Watanni kafin waɗannan muhimman abubuwan da suka faru sun bada dama ga haɗin gwiwar ƙasashe membobi da hukumomi da ƙungiyoyi daban daban don gano hanyoyin da za’a aiwatar da shawarwarin da shiryawa don yin ƙarfaffan haɗin gwiwa tare da haɓaka cigaba ga cigaban muradan ƙarni masu alaƙa da kiwon lafiya na rabin lokacin da SDG ta dauka. Bayanan kula ga Edita:
translations.reference: These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach; engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

**It includes:** Continuing to improve progress on the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) SDG3 Global Action Plan for Health by seeking input from member states on how we coordinate at the country level and responding to recommendations related to it; Managing the GAP as a platform and partnership; Strengthening collaboration at the country level on managing the health authority at the primary level and exploring new issues such as climate-resilient health systems. Utilizing new approaches at the country level to deliver effective systems; UNICEF; Unitaid; Working with member states to empower them through political leadership and leadership and financial support to support partnerships. Key findings from the report will inform discussions at the UN High-Level Political Forum on the SDGs and the Global Health Summit in September 2023. Months leading up to these important events provide opportunities for collaboration between member states, authorities, and various organizations to identify ways to implement recommendations and prepare for strengthening partnerships to advance progress on health-related SDGs within the timeframe that the SDG allows. Notes for Editor:

translations.source: SDG GAP ya bayyana yadda hukumomi goma sha da suka sahannu zasu yi amfani da sabbin hanyoyin aiki, tare. Masu rattaba hannu ga ciga cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG3 GAP sune Gavi, da kuma ƙungiyar allurar rigakafi Kayan Tallafi na Duniya don Mata, Yara da Matasa (GFF); Asusun duniya don yaƙi da cutar AIDS,tarin fuka da zazzaɓin cizon sauro(Asusun duniya) Ƙungiyar ƙwadago ta duniya (ILO); Shirin haɗin gwiwa na majalisar ɗinkin duniya kan HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); Shirin cigaban majalisar ɗinkin duniya (UNDP) Asusun majalisar ɗinkin duniya na yawan jama’a (UNFPA); Asusun Yara na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNICEF); Unitaid; Ƙungiyar majalisar ɗinkin duniya don daidaita jinsi da ƙarfafa mata (matan majalisar ɗinkin duniya)
translations.reference: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO); Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Hausa text:

"The SDG GAP has outlined how ten governments will utilize new approaches together. Signatories to accelerate progress towards the Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 3 GAP are Gavi, and the Vaccine Alliance Global Fund for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); The Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (The Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO); Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women)."

translations.source: Rukunin bankin duniya; Shirn abincin na duniya (WFP); da kuma Hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO). Duba cikakken rahoto me yayi aiki? Menene na gaba?
translations.reference: World Bank Group; World Food Programme (WFP); and World Health Organization (WHO). See full report, What Worked? What’s next?
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa text:

**The World Bank; the World Food Programme (WFP); and the World Health Organization (WHO). How did the comprehensive report perform? What's next?**



**Explanation of Choices:**

*   **Rukunin bankin duniya:** The World Bank
*   **Shirn abincin na duniya (WFP):** World Food Programme (WFP) - I kept the acronym as it's commonly used.
*   **Hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO):** World Health Organization (WHO) - Again, kept the acronym.
*   **Duba cikakken rahoto me yayi aiki?:** How did the comprehensive report perform? (This captures the meaning of "me yayi aiki?" which implies assessing the report's effectiveness or results.)
*   **Menene na gaba?:** What's next?

translations.source: Sabon sakamakon binciken ya nuna tsarin kiwon lafiya ya fara murmurewa da annobar Bayan shekaru uku na cutar COVID-19 tsarin kiwon lafiya a cikin ƙasashe sun fara nuna alamun farko na farfaɗowar tsarin kiwon lafiya a cewar rahoton wucin gadi na hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) game da zagaye na huɗu na binciken bugun jini na duniya kan cigaba da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya a lokacin annobar COVID-19 Nuwaba 2022 January 2023. Ya zuwa farkon shekarar 2023, kasashe sun ba da rahoton samun raguwar cikas wajen isar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya na yau da kullun, amma sun nuna bukatar saka hannun jari don farfadowa da kuma karfin juriya na gaba. A cikin kasashe 139 da suka amsa zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO kasashe sun ba da rahoton ci gaba da wargajewa a kusan kashi daya bisa hudu na ayyuka a matsakaici. A cikin ƙasashe 84 inda za a iya nazarin yanayin, yawan ayyukan da aka katse ya ragu a matsaikata daga 56% a watan Yuli-Satumba 2020 zuwa 23% a watan Nuwamba 2022- Janairu 2023. Ci gaba da rikice-rikicen ya faru ne saboda abubuwan buƙatu- da abubuwan wadata, gami da ƙarancin matakan neman kiwon lafiya a cikin al'ummomi gami da ƙarancin wadatar ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da sauran albarkatun kula da lafiya kamar buɗaɗɗen asibitoci ko samfuran magunguna da samfuran da ake samu. Abin maraba ne cewa tsarin kiwon lafiya a yawancin kasashe sun fara dawo da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya ga miliyoyin mutanen da suka rasa su yayin barkewar cutar," in ji Dr Rudi Eggers, Daraktan Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya. Amma muna buƙatar tabbatar da cewa dukkan ƙasashe suna ci gaba da rufe wannan rata don dawo da ayyukan kiwon lafiya, da kuma amfani da darussan da aka koya don gina shirye-shiryen kiwon lafiya da tsayayye don nan gaba". Alamun farko na samun lafiya A cikin wannan sabon binciken, ƙasashe kaɗan sun ba da rahoton ƙaddamar da damar samun dama ga duk dandamali na isar da sabis da mahimman ayyukan kiwon lafiyar jama'a tun daga rahoton 2020-2021, suna nuna muhimmin mataki na komawa zuw matakin kafin annoba da isar da sabis na da faffadan tsarin aiki.
translations.reference: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023. Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"New survey results show that health systems are beginning to recover from the COVID-19 pandemic. After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries are beginning to show the first signs of health system recovery, according to a recent report by the World Health Organization (WHO) on the fourth round of the global pulse survey on the continuation of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic, November 2022 - January 2023. As of early 2023, countries reported a reduction in disruptions to the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need for investment to revitalize and strengthen resilience for the future.

In the 139 countries that responded to the WHO’s fourth round of the global pulse survey, countries reported a slowdown in service delivery in approximately one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where the situation could be analyzed, the proportion of services interrupted decreased significantly from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022-January 2023. Continued disruptions occurred due to both demand-side and supply-side factors, including low levels of health-seeking behavior in communities, shortages of health workers, and other health resources such as open hospitals or the availability of medicines and supplies.

"It is encouraging that health systems in many countries are beginning to restore essential health services to the millions of people who lost them during the pandemic," said Dr. Rudi Eggers, Director of the World Health Organization’s Health Emergency Programme. "But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to restore health services, and use the lessons learned to build health systems that are resilient for the future."

Early signs of recovery In this new survey, few countries reported expanding access to all service delivery platforms and essential public health services since the 2020-2021 report, indicating a significant step towards returning to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and a functioning system."

translations.source: A ƙarshen 2022, yawancin ƙasashe sun ba da rahoton alamun dawo da sabis na ɓangare, gami da sabis don jima'i, haihuwa, uwa, jarirai, yara da matasa; abinci mai gina jiki; rigakafi; cututtukan cututtuka (gami da zazzabin cizon sauro, HIV, tarin fuka, da sauran cututtukan da ake ɗauka ta hanyar jima'i); cuta mai zafi da aka manta da ita; cututtukan da ba a kamuwa da su; kula da rashin lafiyar taɓin ƙwaƙwalwa, jijiyoyin jiki da kuma amfani da kwayoyi; kula da tsofaffi; da kuma na gargajiya da / ko ƙarin kulawa. Adadin kasashen da ke bayar da rahoton cikas ga tsarin samar da kayayyaki na kasa ya ragu daga kusan rabin (29 daga cikin kasashe 59 da suka amsa) zuwa kusan kashi hudu (18 daga cikin kasashe 66 da suka amsa) a cikin shekarar da ta gabata. Duk da alamun farfadowa, hargitsin sabis na ci gaba da wanzuwa a cikin ƙasashe a duk yankuna da matakan samun kudin shiga, kuma a cikin mafi yawan saitunan isar da sabis da wuraren sabis na ganowa. Ƙasashe kuma suna fuskantar karuwar bayanan sabis - mafi yawan lokuta a cikin sabis don tantancewa, ganowa da kuma magance cututtukan da ba sa yaduwa - wanda zai haifar da mummunan sakamako yayin da mutane ke jinkirta samun kulawa akan lokaci. Farfado da mahimman isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya yana da mahimmanci saboda rikice-rikice - gami da sabis don haɓaka kiwon lafiya, rigakafin cututtuka, gwaje gwajd, jiyya, gyare-gyare - na iya haifar da mummunar illa ga lafiyar jama'a da kuma mutum fiye da annobar kanta, musamman ma. tsakanin jama'a masu rauni. Haɗa ayyukan COVID-19 a cikin mahimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya A wani muhimmin mataki na dawo da tsarin da sauyawa, yawancin ƙasashe sun sami ci gaba wajen haɗa ayyukan COVID-19 a cikin isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum. Kimanin kashi 80-90% na ƙasashe sun haɗa allurar rigakafin COVID-19, bincike da sabis na kula da lamura da kuma sabis don yanayin bayan COVID-19 cikin isar da sabis na yau da kullum. Har yanzu, yawancin ƙasashe (80% na 83 na ƙasashe masu ba da amsa) sun ba da rahoton aƙalla ƙugiya ɗaya don haɓaka damar yin amfani da mahimman kayan aikin COVID-19 (misali COVID-19 bincike, warkewa, alluran rigakafi da kayan kariya na sirri - PPE), tare da batutuwan ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da rashin kuɗi da ke wakiltar mafi yawan shinge. Ƙarin tallafi da ake buƙata don farfadowa, juriya da shirye-shirye Yawancin ƙasashe sun fara amfani da abin da suka koya yayin annobar COVID-19, gami da aiwatar da wasu sabbin dabarun rage rikicewar sabis a cikin isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum.
translations.reference: By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations. Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"At the end of 2022, most countries reported signs of the restoration of essential health services, including services for sexual and reproductive health, childbirth, maternal, newborn, child, and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; sexually transmitted infections (including malaria, HIV, tuberculosis, and other sexually transmitted diseases); neglected tropical diseases; mental health care, substance use and addiction; care for the elderly; and palliative and/or supportive care. The number of countries reporting disruptions to their national health product supply systems decreased from nearly half (29 out of 59 responding countries) to approximately a quarter (18 out of 66 responding countries) in the past year. Despite these signs of recovery, service bottlenecks continue to exist in countries across all regions and income levels, and in most service delivery settings and points of care. Countries are also facing increased service demands – most often in services for diagnosis, detection, and management of non-communicable diseases – which can lead to adverse consequences as people delay seeking care in a timely manner. Rebounding essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including services for health promotion, disease prevention, screening, treatment, and rehabilitation – can have a more severe impact on public and individual health than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations. Integrating COVID-19 activities into essential health services A significant step in the recovery and transformation, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 activities into routine health service delivery. Approximately 80-90% of countries have integrated COVID-19 vaccination, testing, case management, and post-COVID-19 condition services into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) report at least one barrier to improving the availability of essential COVID-19 commodities (e.g., COVID-19 testing, treatment, vaccines, and personal protective equipment - PPE), with workforce and financing challenges representing the most common constraints. Additional support needed for recovery, resilience, and preparedness Many countries have begun leveraging what they learned during the pandemic, including implementing new strategies to mitigate service disruptions in routine health service delivery."

translations.source: Waɗannan sun haɗa da ƙaddamar da hanyoyin sadarwar telemedicine, haɓaka kulawar gida ko ayyukan kulawa da kai, hanyoyin ƙarfafa wadatar ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya, iyawa da hanyoyin tallafi, sabbin abubuwa a cikin samarwa da isar da magunguna da kayayyaki, ƙarin hanyoyin sadarwa na yau da kullun, da haɗin gwiwa tare da kamfanoni masu zaman kansu. masu bayarwa. Kashi uku cikin huɗu na ƙasashe sun ba da rahoton ƙarin tallafin kuɗi don dawo da tsarin dogon lokaci, juriya da shirye shirye. sanarwa ga editoci: A zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na na hukumar lafiya ta duniyaWHO na duniya, an gayyaci kasashe, yankuna da yankuna 222 don amsa daidaitaccen binciken yanar gizo tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 da Janairu 2023. Sanarwa ga editoci: A zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na na hukumar lafiya ta duniyaWHO na duniya, an gayyaci kasashe, yankuna da yankuna 222 don amsa daidaitaccen binciken yanar gizo tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 da Janairu 2023. Binciken na hudu ya biyo bayan binciken hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO na baya na 2020 da 2021: Zagaye na 1 (Mayu-Satumba 2020), Zagaye na 2 (Janairu-Maris 2021), da Zagaye na 3 (Nuwamba-Disamba 2021) wanda ya nuna girman annobar ta shafi ci gaba da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya da kuma yadda kasashe ke daukar matakai. Yayin da binciken bugun jini yake da iyakoki kamar bayar da rahoto na ɓangaranci da wakilci, ƙarfin wannan ƙoƙarin shine yana da cikakke kuma yana ba da bayanai cikin sauri. Yakamata a fahimci kalmar "ƙasa" ta ƙunshi dukkan kasashe, yankuna da wurare. An kammala nazarin abubuwan da ke faruwa bisa la'akari da martani daga ƙasashe 84, yankuna, ko wurare da suka amsa aƙalla sashin bincike ɗaya a cikin duk zagayen binciken bugun jini guda huɗu.
translations.reference: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action. While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

"These include launching telemedicine communication channels, improving home care or self-care activities, ways to strengthen the availability of healthcare workers, capacity and support mechanisms, innovations in the supply and delivery of medicines and supplies, additional communication channels for routine use, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three-quarters of countries have reported increased financial support for long-term system recovery, resilience, and preparedness. Note to editors: In the fourth round of the World Health Organization (WHO) global survey on COVID-19, 222 countries, areas, and territories were invited to respond to a standardized online survey between November 2022 and January 2023. Note to editors: In the fourth round of the World Health Organization (WHO) global survey on COVID-19, 222 countries, areas, and territories were invited to respond to a standardized online survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey follows the WHO’s previous surveys in 2020 and 2021: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021), which showed the extent to which the pandemic affected progress on essential health services and how countries were taking steps. While the COVID-19 survey has limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and provides data quickly. The term "country" should be understood to include all countries, areas, and territories. The analysis of events was completed based on responses from 84 countries, areas, or territories that responded to at least one section of the survey in all four rounds of the COVID-19 survey."

translations.source: Sanarwa na bikin cika shekaru 50 na HRP: Sweden da daular Ingila ta Birtaniya arewacin Ireland Don ɗaukar siyasa mai rai membobin Kwamitin Manufofi da Gudanarwa (PCC) waɗanda ke bikin cika shekaru 50 na HRP da nasarorin da aka samu, Sweden da daular Ingila ta Birtaniya da arewacin Ireland karanta wannan bayanin godiya da taya murna a taron na 36th na HRP. Farashin PCC. Zamaninmu ya sami ci gaba a fannin kimiyya da fasaha fiye da sauran al'ummomin da suka gabata tare kuma sun shaidi sauye-sauye da dama na ilimi, fasaha, likitanci da zamantakewa wanda juyin juya halin hana daukar ciki ya jagoranta sannan kuma ya biyo bayan ci gaban kiwon lafiyar haihuwa, sabbin hanyoyin kiwon lafiya gami da hakkoki da daidaiton jinsi. Kodayake, manyan ƙalubale a fagen SRHR suna ci gaba, kuma, a zahiri, ana sake tambayar wasu ci gaba a yau. Muna yaba wa ma'aikatan HRP-, abokan tarayya da masu goyan baya kan nasarorin da aka samu har zuwa yau kuma mun ga babban ci gaba da aka cimma a cikin rabin karni na samar da shaidun da ke goyan bayan cikakkiyar dama ga lafiyar jima'i da haihuwa da hakki. Muna so mu fayyace manyan nau'ikan nasarori guda uku: Da fari dai, babban bincike na HRP ya yi tasiri mai ma'ana kan manufofin kiwon lafiyar jama'a, ka'idoji, da ayyuka a duk duniya don magance buƙatun ci gaba na al'umma daban-daban. Na biyu, HRP, musamman ta hanyar HRP Alliance, ya ƙarfafa cibiyoyin bincike a cikin gida da kuma na duniya, kuma, ya ba wa masu bincike da yawa damar samun ƙwarewar bincike wanda ke tabbatar da cewa bincike mai inganci na SRHR zai iya ci gaba, kuma ya haɓaka, a nan gaba. Na uku, a cikin duniyar da ke fama da tashin hankali, rikice-rikice da ƙaruwar adawa ga daidaiton jinsi, HRP ta sauƙaƙe kiyaye haƙƙin mata da 'yan mata da kuma al'ummomi masu rauni. HRP ya kasance babban kayan aiki a cikin tsarin Majalisar Dinkin Duniya don bincike game da haifuwar ɗan adam kuma ci gaba da kasancewa jagora musamman a lafiyar jima'i da haihuwa ta duniya.
translations.reference: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.

translations.source: Hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta ƙaddamar da wani sabon shiri don inganta shirye-shiryen annoba Don taimaka wa ƙasashe su shirya don faruwar annoba a gaba,hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta ƙaddamar da wani sabon shiri a yau wanda ke ba da jagora kan haɗaɗɗun tsare-tsare don ba da amsa ga duk wani cututtukan numfashi kamar mura ko coronaviruses. Sabon Tsari da Juriya don Ƙaddamar da wani tsari, ko PRET, ta haɗa sabbin kayan aiki da hanyoyin ilmantarwa da ayyukan gama kai da aka kafa yayin bala'in COVID-19 da sauran abubuwan gaggawa na lafiyar jama'a na baya-bayan nan. Ta hanyar shirin hukumar lafiya ta duniya, WHO za ta yi amfani da tsarin watsa shirye-shirye don jagorantar kasashe a cikin shirye-shiryen annoba, ganin cewa da dama sun zama ruwan dare tsakanin kungiyoyin masu cutar. PRET tana amsa kiran jagorar fasaha da goyan baya don haɓaka da ƙarfafa shirye-shirye a haɗe da amsa, kamar yadda aka tsara a cikin kudurori na Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya. Cutar ta COVID-19 da sauran matsalolin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya sun nuna cewa akwai bukatar kasashe su kasance cikin shiri don magance barazanar kamuwa da cuta, tare da tsare-tsaren shirye-shiryen da aka kera a hannu da ingantacciyar daidaituwa da hadin gwiwa da sauran bangarorin kamar aikin gona. Tsarin shirye-shirye, rigakafi, da mayar da martani ba dole ba ne su kasance lardin da ke bangaren lafiya kaɗai bai,” in ji Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta Janar na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO. Kamar yadda matsalolin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya ke yin tasiri a sassa da dama, haka nan dole ne a shirin mu da kokarin mayar da martani ga ɓangarori, darussa da cututtuka. Yana da mahimmanci kuma, cewa haɗin kai da daidaito tsakanin al'umma su ne cibiyar ƙoƙarinmu, musamman ga waɗanda ba a sani ba kuma galibi suna cikin haɗari. PRET juyin halitta ne a tsarin WHO game da shirye-shiryen kamuwa da cutar ta hanyar aikace-aikacen yanayin watsa ruwan tabarau, maimakon mayar da hankali kan takamaiman cututtuka.
translations.reference: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions. The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English:

The World Health Organization (WHO) has launched a new program to improve pandemic preparedness. To help countries prepare for future outbreaks, the World Health Organization (WHO) launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on comprehensive plans for responding to respiratory illnesses such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Response for Reaching Everyone (PRET) framework incorporates new tools, learning methods, and self-assessment activities established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the WHO program, the organization will use a cascading approach to guide countries in pandemic preparedness, noting that many have become fragmented between responding agencies. PRET responds to the call for technical leadership and support to develop and strengthen response programs in a coordinated manner, as outlined in the World Health Assembly resolutions. The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be prepared to address the threat of disease outbreaks, with well-crafted plans and improved coordination with other sectors such as agriculture. "Preparedness, prevention, and response plans should not be the sole responsibility of the health sector," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO). "As health emergencies impact multiple sectors, our preparedness and response efforts must also encompass those sectors, lessons, and diseases. It is also crucial that collaboration and equity among communities are at the center of our efforts, especially for those who are vulnerable and often at risk." PRET is a paradigm shift in the WHO’s approach to disease preparedness by applying a cascading model, rather than focusing on specific diseases.

translations.source: WHO za ta ci gaba da haɓakawa da yada jagora kan takamaiman cututtuka kamar yadda ake buƙata. Kaddamar da shirin yana gudana ne a rana ta ƙarshe ta tarurruka tare da mutane sama da 120 da ke wakiltar ƙasashe membobin, abokan tarayya, da sakatariyar WHO, don ayyana hanyar ci gaba don PRET, gami da haɓaka taswirar aiwatarwa ta duniya don shirye-shiryen rigakafin cututtukan ƙwayar cuta na numfashi. Tsarin farko na PRET Initiative yana mai da hankali kan ƙwayoyin cuta na numfashi, gami da mura, coronaviruses, da ƙwayar cuta ta syncytial na numfashi. Ganin yadda annobar cutar ta COVID-19 take cigaba da kuma yuwuwar barazanar kamuwa da mura ta jiragen ruwa, wannan tsarin zai baiwa kasashe damar yin nazari sosai, gwadawa, da sabunta yunƙurin shirye-shiryen cutar sankarau don tabbatar da cewa suna da iya aiki da iya aiki a wurin. Ana ci gaba da aiki don gano rukuni na gaba na ƙwayoyin cuta, irin su arboviruses, da za a magance a ƙarƙashin wannan shirin. Wannan zai bi manyan abubuwan da aka gano ta hanyar shawarwari goma don ƙarfafa gine-ginen duniya don shirye-shiryen gaggawa na lafiya, amsawa, da juriya (HEPR). Ƙaddamar da PRET ta haifar da sabon zamani don shirye-shiryen cutar sankara kuma tana wakiltar juyin halitta na ainihin ayyukan hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO don tallafawa duk Membobin Kasashe don ƙarfafa shirye-shiryen gaggawa na kiwon lafiya, rigakafi, da damar amsawa da iyawa. Har ila yau, shirin na iya yin aiki don aiwatar da manufofi da tanade-tanade na Yarjejeniyar Cutar Kwalara, wadda a halin yanzu ƙasashe membobin hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ke tattaunawa. Bayan bayanai da tattaunawa tsakanin mahalarta taron, hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta gayyaci masu ruwa da tsaki zuwa wannan kiran na duniya don aiwatar da shirye-shiryen rigakafin cutar sankarau. Kira zuwa ga Aiki
translations.reference: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative. This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action

WHO will continue to develop and disseminate leadership on addressing infectious diseases as needed. The launch of the program, which took place on the final day of the assembly with over 120 people representing member states, partners, and the WHO Secretariat, was to define a roadmap for PRET, including strengthening the global implementation framework for programs to prevent viral respiratory diseases. The initial focus of the PRET Initiative is on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the potential threat of influenza, this initiative will enable countries to thoroughly review, test, and update their pandemic preparedness plans to ensure they are functional and effective. Work is ongoing to identify the next cohort of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this program. This will build on the key findings from the ten consultations to strengthen global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The launch of PRET marks a new era for pandemic preparedness programs and represents a paradigm shift in the actual work of the global health agency WHO to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency programs, prevention, and enabling response capacity. The program may also work to implement the provisions and principles of the Pandemic Agreement, which WHO Member States are currently negotiating. Following presentations and discussions among participants, the WHO invited stakeholders to this global call to action to implement programs to prevent infectious diseases. Call to Action

translations.source: Hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO, Membobin Kasashe, da abokan haɗin gwiwa suna aiki tare don ganowa da aiwatar da ayyuka masu fifiko don ƙarfafa shirye-shiryen ƙasa, yanki, da duniya don cututtukan cututtuka da kuma barazanar kamuwa da cuta. Mun fahimci rawar al'ummomi da dukkan sassa, da buƙatar daidaitawa da dorewar ci gaba don kawo ƙarshen firgici da sakaci wanda ya zama ruwan dare a cikin shirye-shiryen annoba da martani. Mun tuna da darussa daga bala'i da annoba da suka gabata, musamman wadanda cututtukan numfashi ke haifar da su. Wadannan cututtukan suna kuma za su ci gaba da haifar da babbar barazana ta duniya tare da yuwuwar haifar da mummunar cututtuka da mace-mace, mamaye tsarin kiwon lafiya, dagula tattalin arzikin duniya, da kuma ta'azzara rashin daidaito, wanda ke wanzuwa a cikin damar yin amfani da kayan aikin biyu don hana kamuwa da cuta kiwon lafiya ga dukan mutane. Wannan Kira ne zuwa Aiki don haɓaka shirye-shiryen annoba da barazanar da ke fitowa a duniya. Shiri mai inganci ya dogara ne akan ingantaccen tsari da aiki tare. Alƙawarin gamayya kan ayyuka masu zuwa za su kai ga ci gaban da aka samu nan da Disamba 2025 kamar yadda za a bayyana a cikin tsarin sa ido na (Shiri da Juriya don Barazana masu tasowa (PRET): Sabunta shirye-shiryen da ke tabbatar da ayyukan fifiko kuma waɗanda suka yi la'akari da koyo daga abubuwan da suka faru a baya. Gane haɗarin da ƙwayoyin cuta na numfashi ke haifarwa, tsarawa don da annobar cutar numfashi dangane da jigogin da aka gano a cikin Module PRET #1: Abu ne mai muhimmanci zama cikin shirin fuskantar annobar cututtuka masu alaƙa da numfashi.
translations.reference: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally. Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority

The World Health Organization (WHO), member states, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global programs for infectious diseases and the threat of outbreaks. translations.source: Kara haɗin kai tsakanin masu ruwa da tsaki a cikin shirye-shiryen annoba ta hanyar kyakkyawan tsari da haɗin kai. wannan ya haɗa da samar da tsari na daidaito, da gudanar da ayyuka tare da musayar bayanai game da sahihan hanyoyi da ƙalulabe da damarmaki. Ware kuɗi na musamman, ɗaukar nauyi da shirya bibiyar tasirin annobar da kuma ɗaukar mataki ta la'akari da giɓin da aka samu a yayin wata annoba a baya. matakin da aka ɗauka kan Kwabid-19 'yar manuniya ce game irin nasarar da za a samu daga shugabanci da al'umma da tallafi. daidaito cikin wannan aiki na da muhimmanci domin shiryawa annobar. akwai buƙatar al'umma bakiɗaya ta ɗau matakin da zai tabbatar da nasarar ain da aka sa a gaba. ya kamata aiwatarwar ta ƙarfafi guiwar al'umma wajen gina tabbataccen tsari na yau da kullum da cin moriyar ƙarin dama ta ɗaura ɗamarar ko-ta-kwana. An yi wannan kira ne a lokacin zaburarwa da ɗaura ɗamarar tunƙarar duk wata barazana (PRET): Taron Tattaunawa kan Makomar Annobar Cutar Numfashi ta Duniya da aka yi a ranar 24 ga Afirilu a Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.reference: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding. At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.

"Strengthen coordination between stakeholders in disaster response programs through effective planning and collaboration. This includes establishing a standardized framework, conducting operations with the exchange of data on appropriate methods, estimates, and resources. Allocate dedicated funds, assume responsibility, and prepare to monitor the impact of a disaster, and take actions considering the lessons learned from past disasters. The measures taken regarding COVID-19 serve as a good example of the success that can be achieved through leadership, community engagement, and support. Equity in this preparedness effort is crucial. There is a need for the entire community to take action to ensure the achievement of the set goals. Implementation should empower the community to build a robust system for everyday preparedness and benefit from additional opportunities to address immediate needs. This call was made during a high-level dialogue on pandemic preparedness, the Pandemic Preparedness and Response Dialogue held on April 24th in Geneva, Switzerland."

translations.source: Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta yi kira da a ƙara yawan yadda ake amfani da abubuawan da za su taimaka wajen yaƙi da zazzaɓin cizon sauro a yayin Ranar Yaƙi da Zazzaɓin Cizon Sauro ta Duniya, Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta yi kira da a ƙara ƙoƙari wajen amfani da sabbi da matakan da ake da su da wajen ceto rayuka daga zazzaɓin cizon sauro. Yara kusan miliyan 1.5 da ke fuskantar barazanar kamuwa ko rasa rai sabili da cutar maleriya daga ƙasashen Ghana, Kenya da Malawi, sun samu kashin farko na allurar rigakafin cutar maleriya RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S). Godiya ga shirin na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya. kashin farko na rigakafin maleriya wanda aka ƙaddamar a 2019 na cigaba da samar da hanyoyin kariya al'umma da kuma ceton rayuka. idan aka faɗaɗa shirin, Hukumar Lafiya ta duniya na ganin za a iya kai wa ga ceto rayukan dubun nan yara a kowacce shekara. a cewar darakta janar na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO), muna da kayan kai ɗauki da za mu iya rage tasirin cutar maleriya da suka haɗa da: abin magance ƙwayoyin yaɗa cutar da magugunan rigakafi da na gwaje-gwaje da na maganin cutar. waɗannan sun ƙunshi ingantattun rigakafin cutar maleriya da za su kai ga ceton dubun nan yara a kowacce shekara. akwai yuwuwar kawo ƙarshen cutar maleriya a ƙasashe da dama, idan aka ƙara himma wajen cigaba da kai ɗauki ga waɗanda suka fi fuskantar baranaza. An yi wa ranar cutar maleriya ta duniya ta 2023 take da lokacin kawo ƙarshen maleriya bakiɗaya: zuba jari, fikira da ɗaukar mataki. Taken, wata hanya ce da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ke son yin kira wajen amfani da kayan aiki da sahihan hanyoyi domin hana kamuwa da ba da magungunan magance maleriya musamman a tsakanin talakawa.
translations.reference: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year. “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.hypothesis:

The World Health Organization (WHO) has called for increased use of tools to combat malaria during World Malaria Day. The WHO is urging greater efforts in utilizing existing and new measures to save lives from malaria. Approximately 1.5 million children at risk of contracting or losing their lives to malaria in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi have received the first dose of the malaria vaccine RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S). Thanks to the World Health Organization's program. The first dose of the malaria vaccine, launched in 2019, continues to provide community protection and save lives. If the program is expanded, the World Health Organization believes it could save tens of thousands of children's lives each year. According to the Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO), we have tools at our disposal to reduce the impact of malaria, including mosquito control measures, preventative and experimental drugs, and treatments for the disease. These include improved malaria prevention methods that could save tens of thousands of children each year. There is the possibility of eradicating malaria in several countries if more effort is put into reaching those most at risk. The 2023 World Malaria Day is themed "End Malaria Together: Invest, Innovate, and Implement." This is a call by the World Health Organization to utilize effective tools and strategies to prevent malaria and provide treatment, especially among vulnerable populations.
